Innovative Mark Ford Rhapsody Series Concerto Medium Marimba Mallets are designed to deliver a brighter more articulate sound than other Mark Ford Mallets. The Medium Hard Mallet is perfect for the entire range of the instrument. It functions in any roll in a graduated set or as a solo mallet.

Medium hard mallet core
Birch handles

Name: Innovative Ivan Trevino Medium Bright Marimba Mallets - Walnut Stained Birch
Description:
The Innovative Ivan Trevino Medium Bright Marimba mallets features a core tailored for a distinct sound and balance. This mallet functions a an all purpose mallet delivering a bright sound with a clean fundamental. The marimba models feature longer shafts for ease of playing wide intervals.

Handles - Walnut stained birch
Synthetic Core
Red tape indicator on the handle
Length - 16.75"

Name: Innovative Sandi Rennick Series Soft Marimba Mallets
Description:
Constructed with uniquely weighted compact cores, the IP4000 series marimba mallets were designed to the specifications of Sandi Rennick, the Front Ensemble Arranger of the 9-time DCI World Champion Santa Clara Vanguard. Putting more weight in the players hands, these models produce a focused sound which speaks quickly, and was created with today's modern amplified keyboard section in mind.

Starting with durable birch handles in a matte finish, these implements are also wrapped with synthetic yarn which makes them able to withstand the needs of rigorous rehearsal and performance schedules. The versatility found in the Sandi Rennick series makes balancing and blending your ensemble easier than ever before.

Sandi Rennick Series
IP4001: Soft Marimba
IP4001C: Soft Marimba - Cord
IP4002: Medium Marimba
IP4002C: Medium Marimba - Cord
IP4002.5: Medium Hard Marimba
IP4002.5C: Medium Hard Marimba - Cord
IP4003: Hard Marimba
IP4003C: Hard Marimba - Cord
IP4004: Wrapped Xylophone
IP4005: Soft Vibraphone
IP4006: Medium Vibraphone
IP4007: Hard Vibraphone

Name: Innovative Mark Ford IP814 Hard Birch Marimba Mallets
Description: The IP814 from the Innovative percussion Mark Ford Strong legato series is made from a rubber core wrapped in soft yarn and set atop an finished birch handle. This mallet is the Hard mallet of the series and will excel at fast solo performances or as a soprano mallet in a graduated set. It lives up to its name and produces strong fundamentals and strong legato.

Name: Innovative Fundamental Series F8 Medium Rubber Marimba Mallets
Description:
Innovative Percussion F8 Medium Rubber Marimba Mallets feature black painted birch handles.

Manufactured by the leader in high quality keyboard mallets, the Innovative Percussion Fundamental Series Keyboard Mallets were specifically designed with the beginning percussionist and budget conscious band director in mind. This economical series offers the beginning percussionist and band director a line of quality keyboard mallets at an affordable price.

The Fundamental Series is available in 15 different models. All models feature black birch dowels and the two vibraphone models are also available with high quality rattan handles.

Name: Encore NM3B Nanae Mimura Medium Hard Birch Marimba Mallets
Description: Nanae Mimura Series mallets are slightly heavier than NZ Series mallets. They feature rubber disk cores hand wrapped with latex, and are covered by North American wool. Encore's unique hand wrapped latex process enhances the fundamental allowing harder mallets which do not sound harsh, and improves the feel of the mallet's rebound. The yarn is all natural wool which is not chemically processed and therefore particularly soft and durable. (Our yarn is purchased from the sheep farm that harvests the wool - the yarn is so natural that you may occasionally see a small piece of hay from a mallet head!) The softness of their yarn combined with the hardness and clarity of their latex-wrapped disk cores make NM Series mallets two-toned and very versatile. Medium hard, with birch shafts.

Name: Vic Firth Robert Van Sice Keyboard Mallets - Hard
Description:
The Robert van Sice Signature Keyboard mallets from Vic Firth feature a heavy synthetic core that produces a very robust and complete sound. These yarn wound marimba mallets have been crafted with an exclusive hand-wrapping technique that virtually eliminates the sound of bar contact. A special interlocking stitch pattern maintains the integrity of the head. The maple handles are 17″ long and provide an excellent feel and control. With a full depth of sound and excellent versatility of color, these mallets are the perfect choice for music from Japanese repertoire and pieces demanding multiple layers of timbre.

The M126 hard mallets are a great choice for moments when the player needs maximum cutting power and edge to the sound.

Name: Malletech MTech Marimba Mallets - Soft
Description: These are the Soft Marimba Mallets from the MTech Mallet Series functioning perfectly in the lower octaves of instrument. These mallets are designed to pair perfectly with the MTech Marimba series.

Name: Innovative Pius Cheung PIUS5B Hard Marimba Mallets - Birch
Description:
The Innovative Percussion Pius Cheung Series Marimba Mallets features six models with rubber cores of varying hardness. Constructed on Birch handles, each model is wrapped with a very unique alpaca-blend yarn that allows the player to produce musically strong fundamental at extreme dynamic ranges from the utmost silky pianissimo to peasante fortissimo with minimal effort.

The PIUS5 is a hard, high-range mallet for ensemble or concerto playing.

Name: Encore NZ1B Nancy Zeltsman Hard Birch Marimba Mallets
Description:
The Encore NZ1B Nancy Zeltsman "Hard" mallets are very bright, and ideal for cutting through while retaining a "marimba tone" (as opposed to a xylophone tone). They sound particularly clear and beautiful for soft playing in the highest register. Birch Handles.

One unique characteristic of my line is that the oval-shape of the mallet head is horizontal rather than vertical. I imagine (and maybe it's true) that the width of the mallet activates more of the vertical grains of wood in every bar-thus contributing to a richer tone. Everything about the construction of my line of mallets-from the cores, to the latex, to the type of yarn-is aimed at helping you to produce a fundamental-rich tone.

- Nancy Zeltsman

Hardness - Hard
Handles - Birch

Name: Malletech Leigh Howard Stevens LS1 Very Soft Birch Marimba Mallets
Description:
Very soft, single-toned bass mallet for effortless, continuous organ-style chorale playing.

The nine models in the Stevens line are the smoothest rolling, least “slappy” mallets for solo marimba playing available anywhere in the world. Each model has four to six distinct layers of construction which work with the player to generate the maximum bar sound, minimize contact noise and offer superb low-dynamic control, all without sacrificing any of the high volume potential of a “normal” mallet. These layers of construction and performance features are built into a mallet that is still reasonably lightweight, perfectly balanced and therefore easy to maneuver through today’s challenging solo literature. All Leigh Howard Stevens Mallets feature super soft yarn and select birch handles.

Name: Marimba One Double Helix DHR2 Hard Rattan Mallets
Description:
The Double Helix Series was developed to bring out the warmth of the fundamental throughout the range of the 5-octave marimba. The relaxed yarn tension on these mallets enhances the smooth, dark attack of the mallet.

The exclusive patent pending &#34double helix wrap&#34 technique allows you to easily identify each mallet in the series. Two strands of yarn are wrapped independently of each other, creating a woven effect. After one of the strands makes a revolution around the mallet head, the other strand makes a revolution, securing the previous strand. This locks the yarn in place and prevents it from unraveling. This unique method of wrapping provides our mallets with their distinct look, and exceptional sound.

Name: Innovative Mark Ford IP811 Soft Birch Marimba Mallets
Description: Innovative Percussion expands its Mark ford series with the IP811 marimba mallet. This is part of Ford's Strong Legato series. This uses a slightly larger rubber core to produce a stronger fundamental while the extra soft yarn lessens the bar contact sound. Perfect as a bass mallet in a graduated set or solo mallet when the performance calls for a softer touch.

Name: Innovative William Moersch IP502 Med Soft Marimba Mallets
Description:
This four model series of mallets has been designed exclusively for marimba virtuoso William Moersch. The hard acrylic-style core offers a full sound at soft dynamic levels and a bright sound at louder dynamic levels.

Features:
-Hard acrylic-style cores to create a full, bright tone and rich fundamental
-100% imported wool yarn
-Polished, hand-selected birch handles

Name: Vic Firth Rosauro Keyboard Mallets - Medium Hard Marimba
Description:
Vic Firth M223 Ney Rosauro Medium Hard Marimba Mallets produce a versatile and general mallet that produces full and natural sound throughout the entire keyboard. With rattan shafts.

Ney Rosauro designed this special series of marimba mallets to reflect the sound he desires for his award winning compositions. Rattan shafts and rubber cores coupled with special yarn and cord choices assure the player of a pure and natural sound that projects the full capabilities of the instrument.

Length: 16 3/4"

Name: Malletech Concerto Series CN21 Hard to Very Hard Birch Marimba Mallets
Description:
The CN21 is a hard to very hard mallet that still rolls well at low dynamic levels. Hard to Very Hard- Multi-Toned.

The six models that comprise the Concerto Series have been designed specifically for those players looking for a heavy mallet with a big sound that will project through a large ensemble, but do not want the contact noise that is usually associated with this type of mallet. Compared to standard traditional mallets of similar hardness, the player will immediately notice proportionally more fundamental tone and greater volume. While many synthetic yarns suffer from high-pitched “tick”, this yarn is very quiet on the attack, while exceeding the durability and abrasion resistance of nylon.

Name: Malletech Kevin Bobo KB14 Hard Birch Marimba Mallets
Description: Kevin Bobo is currently serving as Assistant Professor of Percussion at The University of Kansas. He has performed nationally and internationally as a solo recitalist, clinician, and orchestral percussionist. He holds a Bachelors Degree from Wichita State University where he studied with J.C. Combs and a Masters Degree from Ithaca College where he studied with Gordon Stout.

Name: Innovative Soloist Series IP400 Hard Birch Marimba Mallets
Description:
The Innovative Percussion IP400 Soloist Series Hard Marimba Mallets are designed for use in the upper 3 octaves of a 5 octave marimba and offers lots of strong articuation. This model features 100% wool yarn, birch handles and hard rubber cores that help to create a full, rich tome and warm fundamental.

Designed with the marimba virtuoso in mind, the Soloist Series is constructed using hard rubber cores, covered in a series of latex combinations. The mallets are wrapped is a 100% wool wrap, custom manufactured specifically for Innovative Percussion, which delivers a brilliant, rich tone with the warmest fundamental possible in all registers of the marimba. The series is offered on birch handles that are polished for the smoothest natural finish. Each step of production insures a mallet designed to meet the highest standards of today's most demanding marimbist.

Name: Innovative Pius Cheung PIUS4 Medium Hard Marimba Mallets
Description:
The Innovative Percussion Pius Cheung Series Marimba Mallets features six models with rubber cores of varying hardness. Constructed on rattan handles, each model is wrapped with a very unique alpaca-blend yarn that allows the player to produce a tone of gold at extreme dynamic ranges from the utmost silky pianissimo to peasante fortissimo with minimal effort.

The PIUS4 is a medium hard, high-range mallet for solo playing.

Name: Marimba One Lynn Vartan LVB3 Medium Birch Marimba Mallets
Description:
The Marimba One Lynn Vartan LVB3 Medium Birch Marimba Mallet features a medium round head and is medium weight. Designed for warmth and articulation, this mallet becomes your "go to" mallet for any repertoire or venue. Birch handles.

The Marimba One Lynn Vartan LVB3 Medium Birch Marimba Mallets function very well across the entire marimba. Lynn Vartan series mallets are "just right" for any grip, any piece, any ensemble, or any venue. The incredibly durable and warm-sounding yarns give clarity, articulation and depth. This versatile series covers your needs from the combo vibe/marimba mallets at the hard end of the range down to the super warm and rich soft mallet. You're sure to find your "just right" fit in this line to add to your quiver!

Name: Marimba One Round Sound Birch Marimba Mallets - Medium Soft
Description:
Marimba One Round Sound mallets are the perfect marriage of Marimba One sound quality and the needs of percussionists. The RSB4 is a medium soft mallet, lightweight yet produces a rich and articulate bass sound, with a birch handle.

Marimba One Round Sound series mallets feature the same Marimba One excellence at a more affordable price. They are a lightweight mallet with warmth and articulation. This is the mallet that works to make you sound great. Double-wound using Marimba One’s exclusive technology.

Name: Encore NZ3R Nancy Zeltsman Medium Hard Rattan Marimba Mallets
Description:
The Encore NZ3B Nancy Zeltsman "Medium Hard" mallets are the most versatile mallets in the line. They sound excellent in all registers, particularly the middle of the instrument (from about C3 to C6). As a side note, NZ3's are especially valuable to have in your collection at different levels of wear. An old NZ3 is essentially an "NZ2.5"! Rattan Handles.

One unique characteristic of my line is that the oval-shape of the mallet head is horizontal rather than vertical. I imagine (and maybe it's true) that the width of the mallet activates more of the vertical grains of wood in every bar-thus contributing to a richer tone. Everything about the construction of my line of mallets-from the cores, to the latex, to the type of yarn-is aimed at helping you to produce a fundamental-rich tone.

- Nancy Zeltsman

Hardness - Medium Hard
Handles - Rattan

Name: Innovative Artisan Series - Medium Hard Marimba Mallet - Cedar
Description:
The Innovative Artisan Series Medium Hard Marimba Mallet functions across the entire instrument delivering fast articulate tonalities. The medium hardness of this mallet makes this perfect when the performance requires fast and defined lower registrar notes.

Medium hard graduated rubber core
Cedar handles

Name: Innovative Weichen Lin Marimba Mallets Birch - Medium Soft
Description:
The Innovative Weichen Lin Medium Soft Marimba Mallets function perfectly on the lower octaves of the instrument allowing you to achieve clear fundamentals and with some added punch. The blended yarn creates an incredibly versatile mallet, capable of lush Bach chorales as well as more contemporary articulate passages. Built on birch shafts, the rubber cores provide a strong fundamental tone with very little contact sound.

-16 3/4" Total Length

Name: Innovative Thomas Burritt TB4 Medium Hard Marimba Mallets
Description:
The Innovative Thomas Burritt TB4 Marimba Mallet is the medium hard mallet of the series. Designed specifically for the upper 3 octaves of the marimba, the TB4 features a quick but legato attack. Even with the hardness of the mallet core the loosely wrapped soft yarn will help control the attack and timbre. Special attention is paid to the balance of the mallet to create a unique experience for the performer.

Medium hard synthetic mallet core
Birch wood handles with a walnut stain
Loosely wrapped core to control attack and enhance fundamental resonance

Name: Innovative Tom Rarick IP3002 Medium Soft Birch Marimba Mallets
Description:
While the contributions of the front ensemble continue to evolve within the marching idiom, so do the demands of the mallets that are used by their highly talented players. Tom Rarick, percussion arranger for the Bluecoats Drum & Bugle Corps, has assisted Innovative Percussion with the design of this unique series of keyboard mallets.

The Tom Rarick Series marimba mallets from Innovative Percussion feature large round cores wrapped with a light grey synthetic wool-blend yarn, while the vibraphone mallets have cord-wrapped large oval cores. All 7 models are built on slightly larger (11/32") polished birch handles. The heavier cores increase projection and enhance the fundamental tone, while the larger handles help balance and control.

IP3001 - Soft Marimba
IP3002 - Medium Soft Marimba
IP3003 - Medium Hard Marimba
IP3004 - Hard Marimba
IP3005 - Soft Vibraphone
IP3006 - Medium Vibraphone
IP3007 - Hard VIbraphone

Name: Vic Firth Virtuoso Series Keyboard Mallets - Hard
Description:
Vic Firth M214 Virtuoso Series Hard Marimba Mallets are designed for repertoire that requires superb articulation.

Designed for the solo marimba performer, the Vic Firth Virtuoso Series features a rubber core wrapped with a wool blend yarn for an enhanced warm and full-bodied sound. The handles are made of birch with a unique "slip-resistant" finish for excellent feel and enhanced control. A fine choice in ensemble settings as well.

Length: 17"

Name: Innovative Percussion Sandi Rennick Series Medium Marimba Mallets - Sage Green Cord
Description:
The IP4000C series features cord-wound marimba mallets designed to the specifications of Sandi Rennick, the Front Ensemble Arranger of the 9-time DCI World Champion Santa Clara Vanguard. Known for putting more weight in the players hands, these models produce a focused sound which speaks quickly, and was created with today's modern amplified keyboard section in mind. The addition of a cord-wrapped option adds enhanced articulation and a new voice for the front ensemble.

Starting with durable birch handles in a matte finish, these implements are wrapped with cord allowing them to be articulate and cutting while withstanding the needs of rigorous rehearsal and performance schedules. The versatility found in the Sandi Rennick series makes balancing and blending your ensemble easier than ever before.

Sandi Rennick Series
IP4001: Soft Marimba
IP4001C: Soft Marimba - Cord
IP4002: Medium Marimba
IP4002C: Medium Marimba - Cord
IP4002.5: Medium Hard Marimba
IP4002.5C: Medium Hard Marimba - Cord
IP4003: Hard Marimba
IP4003C: Hard Marimba - Cord
IP4004: Wrapped Xylophone
IP4005: Soft Vibraphone
IP4006: Medium Vibraphone
IP4007: Hard Vibraphone

Name: Innovative Sandi Rennick Series Hard Marimba Mallets
Description:
Constructed with uniquely weighted compact cores, the IP4000 series marimba mallets were designed to the specifications of Sandi Rennick, the Front Ensemble Arranger of the 9-time DCI World Champion Santa Clara Vanguard. Putting more weight in the players hands, these models produce a focused sound which speaks quickly, and was created with today's modern amplified keyboard section in mind.

Starting with durable birch handles in a matte finish, these implements are also wrapped with synthetic yarn which makes them able to withstand the needs of rigorous rehearsal and performance schedules. The versatility found in the Sandi Rennick series makes balancing and blending your ensemble easier than ever before.

Sandi Rennick Series
IP4001: Soft Marimba
IP4001C: Soft Marimba - Cord
IP4002: Medium Marimba
IP4002C: Medium Marimba - Cord
IP4002.5: Medium Hard Marimba
IP4002.5C: Medium Hard Marimba - Cord
IP4003: Hard Marimba
IP4003C: Hard Marimba - Cord
IP4004: Wrapped Xylophone
IP4005: Soft Vibraphone
IP4006: Medium Vibraphone
IP4007: Hard Vibraphone

Name: Liberty One LMH Hard Yarn Birch Marimba Mallets
Description:
The Liberty One Series Marimba Mallets are available in three different head types for all of your playing situations and are great mallets at a great price!

This version is the LMH Hard Yarn model and works well for loud musical passages, particularly in the middle and upper register of the instrument.

Name: Malletech Go2 Series Andy Harnsberger Graduated Marimba Mallet Set
Description:
The Malletech Andy Harnsberger Go2 Series Marimba Mallets offer a slightly graduated set of four that sounds great all over the instrument. There is just enough difference between them to take out the harshness in the bottom octave of the instrument, but still have sufficient pop in the top octave.

Sold in graduated set of 4 mallets.

Name: Innovative Pius Cheung PIUS1B Soft Bass Marimba Mallets - Birch
Description:
The Innovative Percussion Pius Cheung Series Marimba Mallets features six models with rubber cores of varying hardness. Constructed on Birch handles, each model is wrapped with a very unique alpaca-blend yarn that allows the player to produce solid fundamental tone at extreme dynamic ranges from the utmost silky pianissimo to peasante fortissimo with minimal effort.

The PIUS1 is a soft, powerful bass mallet for solo playing.

Name: Innovative Ludwig Albert IP3104 Medium Soft Rattan Marimba Mallets
Description:
This line of professional keyboard mallets has been specifically designed for Belgium marimba artist Ludwig Albert III. The eight models feature shafts constructed of superior quality rattan and specially weighted rubber cores of varying sizes and degrees of hardness. Wool-blend yarn is used to fully maximize the dynamic potential of the line, while the unique shape allows for precise tonal clarity. The length provides an ultimate balance, which will meet the needs of today's professional player.

IP3101 Extra Soft Marimba
IP3102 Medium Extra Soft Marimba
IP3103 Soft Marimba
IP3104 Medium Soft Marimba
IP3105 Medium Marimba
IP3106 Medium Hard Marimba
IP3107 Hard Marimba
IP3108 Extra Hard Marimba

Features:

Rubber cores of varying size, shape, and hardness

Superior quality rattan

Wool-blend yarn wrap

Name: Innovative Mark Ford Rhapsody Series - IP821 Medium Marimba Mallets
Description:
Innovative Mark Ford Rhapsody Series Concerto Medium Marimba Mallets are designed to deliver a brighter more articulate sound than other Mark Ford Mallets. The Medium Mallet is perfect for the entire range of the instrument. It functions in any roll in a graduated set or as a solo mallet.

Medium hardness
Birch handles

Name: Innovative She-e Wu WU2 Medium Soft Birch Marimba Mallets
Description:
The She-e Wu Signature Series Marimba Mallets from Innovative Percussion feature seven mallets of varying hardness, with natural birch handles and a custom blended yarn wrap. Each captures the unique style, sensitivity and essence of the artist that is She-e Wu.

Features:
A combination of hard acrylic-style and hard rubber cores to create a full, bright tone and rich fundamental
Hand-selected unfinished natural birch handles
Wool-blend yarn wrap
Elongated shaped mallet heads

Name: Vic Firth Virtuoso Series Keyboard Mallets - Medium Soft
Description:
Vic Firth M211 Virtuoso Series Medium Soft Marimba Mallets produce a full-bodied sound that still affords a degree of clarity.

Designed for the solo marimba performer, the Vic Firth Virtuoso Series features a rubber core wrapped with a wool blend yarn for an enhanced warm and full-bodied sound. The handles are made of birch with a unique "slip-resistant" finish for excellent feel and enhanced control. A fine choice in ensemble settings as well.

Length: 17"

Name: Vic Firth Robert Van Sice Keyboard Mallets - Very Soft
Description:
The Robert van Sice Signature Keyboard mallets from Vic Firth feature a heavy synthetic core that produces a very robust and complete sound. These yarn wound marimba mallets have been crafted with an exclusive hand-wrapping technique that virtually eliminates the sound of bar contact. A special interlocking stitch pattern maintains the integrity of the head. The maple handles are 17″ long and provide an excellent feel and control. With a full depth of sound and excellent versatility of color, these mallets are the perfect choice for music from Japanese repertoire and pieces demanding multiple layers of timbre.

The M121 is a very soft mallet that produces a heavy and dark sound. These are perfect for the bass range of the marimba.

Name: Malletech Blake Tyson BT16 Marimba Mallets - Medium to Hard
Description:
The new line of Blake Tyson mallets offer great expressive potential. They respond to subtle changes of touch, and their depth of sound and sensitive response help to create an amazing variety of timbres and articulation. The Blake Tyson mallets are made with durable wood blend yarn on birch handles.

The BT16 provides a warm tone across the entire instrument while allowing the performer subtle control of timbre and articulation. They are the perfect all-around mallet. Medium to hard.

Name: Innovative Pius Cheung PIUS2 Medium Soft Bass Marimba Mallets
Description:
The Innovative Percussion Pius Cheung Series Marimba Mallets features six models with rubber cores of varying hardness. Constructed on rattan handles, each model is wrapped with a very unique alpaca-blend yarn that allows the player to produce a strong fundamental tone at extreme dynamic ranges from the utmost silky pianissimo to peasante fortissimo with minimal effort.

The PIUS2 is a medium soft bass mallet for ensemble or concerto playing.

Name: Innovative Fundamental Series F7 Soft Rubber Marimba Mallets
Description:
Innovative Percussion F7 Soft Rubber Marimba Mallets feature black painted birch handles.

Manufactured by the leader in high quality keyboard mallets, the Innovative Percussion Fundamental Series Keyboard Mallets were specifically designed with the beginning percussionist and budget conscious band director in mind. This economical series offers the beginning percussionist and band director a line of quality keyboard mallets at an affordable price.

The Fundamental Series is available in 15 different models. All models feature black birch dowels and the two vibraphone models are also available with high quality rattan handles.

Name: Marimba One Katarzyna Mycka KMB5 Medium Soft Birch Mallets
Description:
The Katarzyna Mycka Professional Mallet Series are wrapped very tightly with all synthetic yarn. The combination of synthetic yarn and the tight wrap gives the immediate response of the bar that is required for ensemble and orchestra playing. The heavy weight of these mallets brings out the warmth of the bars by physically putting into motion the fundamental frequency of the bars.

Katarzyna recommends using this medium soft mallet for such pieces as Anna Ignatowicz's Toccata; Ney Rosauro's Concerto No. 2 (2nd Mvmt); Minoru Miki's Marimba Spiritual (opening part).

Name: Innovative Weichen Lin Marimba Mallets Birch - Medium Hard
Description:
The Innovative Weichen Lin Medium Hard Marimba Mallets function perfectly across the entire instrument allowing you to achieve clear fundamentals with a faster attack. The blended yarn creates an incredibly versatile mallet, capable of lush Bach chorales as well as more contemporary articulate passages. Built on birch shafts, the rubber cores provide a strong fundamental tone with very little contact sound.

-16 3/4" Total Length

Name: Innovative She-e Wu WU5C Hard Concerto Birch Marimba Mallets
Description: The WU5C Hard Concerto models are an addition to the She-e Wu Series marimba mallets. This model is constructed much like the existing WU5, but is tightly wrapped with cord. This helps produce a bright, staccato timbre that speaks very quickly and will help cut through a large ensemble.

Name: Yamaha Keiko Abe MKA-04 Hard Rattan Marimba Mallets
Description:
The MKA04 from the Yamaha Keiko Abe Mallet Series is also a hard mallet but lighter in feel than the MKA03. The MKA04 features a rubber core wrapped with a 1mm rubber tube. The Shaft on the MKA04 is 430mm and the mallet is finished with a high quality yarn.

This mallet will excel as a soprano mallet in a graduated set. Perfect for the upper octaves of the marimba.

Name: Marimba One Wave Wrap WWXR3 Medium Rattan Mallets
Description: The WWXR3 is a medium mallet with Gray/Blaze colored yarn and rattan handles. The highest concentration of yarn is wrapped around the mallet's equator, creating an increased striking surface and a mallet that is more responsive than traditionally wrapped mallets. The Wave Wrap's blend of wool and nylon yarn minimizes contact sound and enhances articulation. Using Wave Wrap technology, the mallet is balanced to facilitate the ease of movement in the player's hand. The double equatorial wrap creates a chevron pattern around the playing surface and a distinctive wave on top of the head - two unique visual signatures for these finest of marimba mallets.

Name: Innovative David Friedman DF25 Rattan Marimba Mallets
Description: The David Friedman Series vibraphone and marimba mallets were designed for internationally acclaimed percussionist, composer, and educator, David Friedman. These marimba mallets are cord-wrapped and built on rattan handles. The DF25 is a versatile mallet that posesses a wide palette of colors, speaking fully in all registers. It is perfect in an ensemble setting.

Name: Malletech Kevin Bobo KB8 Soft Birch Marimba Mallets
Description: Kevin Bobo is currently serving as Assistant Professor of Percussion at The University of Kansas. He has performed nationally and internationally as a solo recitalist, clinician, and orchestral percussionist. He holds a Bachelors Degree from Wichita State University where he studied with J.C. Combs and a Masters Degree from Ithaca College where he studied with Gordon Stout.

Name: Innovative Thomas Burritt TB1 Soft Marimba Mallets
Description:
The Innovative Thomas Burritt TB1 Marimba Mallet is the soft mallet of the series. This makes it perfect for chorale pieces or the lowest registrar of a 5 octave marimba. A synthetic core sits on top of a Ramin wood handle. The mallet head is loosely wrapped and balanced to give this mallet the ability to control timbre and minimize attack.

Soft synthetic core
Birch wood handles with a walnut stain
Loosely wrapped yarn to minimize attack and control timbre

Name: Innovative Rattan Series RS40 Hard Marimba/Vibraphone Mallets
Description:
This Innovative Rattan Series Hard Marimba/Vibraphone Mallet is designed for the percussion ensemble or contemporary mallet player. A tightly yarn wrapped graduated rubber core allows this mallet to function on mallet instruments as well as other accessory percussion.
- Matched Rattan Handles

Name: Salyers Etude Series Birch Marimba Mallets - Soft
Description:
The Salyers Percussion E10 soft yarn mallet features birch shafts, a rubber core and high quality wool/nylon blend yarn for a full, round tone.

The Etude Series is designed to be a high quality and economical solution for percussionists and band directors. The Etude Series is made with the same quality materials that you will find on many higher priced alternatives. The design, craftsmanship and material selection set this series apart from other “value” brands on the market today.

Name: Malletech Mtech Marimba Mallets - Hard
Description: These are the Hard Marimba Mallets from the MTech Mallet Series great for the upper range of the instrument. These mallets are designed to pair perfectly with the MTech Marimba series.

Name: Encore GRADR Nancy Zeltsman Graduated Rattan Mallet Set
Description:
The Encore Nancy Zeltsman Graduated Set was devised to provide an affordable means to try a graduated set if you haven't already done so. This is a combination of mallets I use about 60% of the time. It naturally "voices" most chord playing. In essence, this graduated set is an example of the way my entire line was designed to best flatter different registers of the instrument.

This Graduated Set of 4 Encore Nancy Zeltsman's mallets includes 1 each of NZ2R Medium Harder, NZ3R Medium Hard, NZ4R Medium and NZ6R Articulate Bass. Rattan Handles.

One unique characteristic of my line is that the oval-shape of the mallet head is horizontal rather than vertical. I imagine (and maybe it's true) that the width of the mallet activates more of the vertical grains of wood in every bar-thus contributing to a richer tone. Everything about the construction of my line of mallets-from the cores, to the latex, to the type of yarn-is aimed at helping you to produce a fundamental-rich tone.

-Nancy Zeltsman

Name: Malletech Michael Burritt Ensemble Series Marimba Mallets - Medium Hard MBE13
Description:
Malletech Michael Burritt Ensemble Series Medium Hard Marimba Mallets offer a strong and punchy sound yet not overly bright. Perfect for mid to high range of the instrument.

Being one of the harder mallets of the series, these offer clarity and transparency with a bit of bite. The birch handles and weighting of the mallets have a nice ‘throw’ that allows you to play though the bar with ease, resulting in a bigger sound with less effort.

The Michael Burritt Ensemble Series is an incredibly versatile series designed to excel in musical settings ranging from solo, to chamber, and ensembles of all sizes. The mallets deliver a warm, woody tone that is robust and round while still providing the strong articulation required to cut through the ensemble.

Synthetic Yarn
Medium Hard Hardness
Birch Shafts
Acorn Shaped Mallet Head

Name: Salyers Earth Tone Birch Marimba Mallets - Medium
Description:
The Earth Tone Series ET30 medium yarn mallet works well as a legato for the alto voice. The attack is round and warm yet defined and not blurry. The natural birch shafts offer superior control to the performer.

The Salyers Percussion Earth Tone Series drew its inspiration from the warm sounds of natural wood and metallic instruments. The yarn wrapped models feature natural birch mallets, 100% soft wool yarn and round rubber cores that create warm and rich organic tones. The rattan models have oval rubber cores and are wrapped in a super durable hemp cord. The Hemp allows these mallets to have a big fundamental tone and the unmistakable sound of a hemp cord wrap.

Name: Innovative Weichen Lin Marimba Mallets Birch - Soft
Description:
The Innovative Weichen Lin Soft Marimba Mallets function perfectly on the bass notes on the instrument allowing you to achieve clear fundamentals and smooth rolls. The blended yarn creates an incredibly versatile mallet, capable of lush Bach chorales as well as more contemporary articulate passages. Built on birch shafts, the rubber cores provide a strong fundamental tone with very little contact sound.

-16 3/4" Total Length

Name: Innovative Dan Moore DM21 Medium Vibe/Marimba Mallet
Description:
This series of marimba and vibraphone mallets was developed for musician, composer and educator Dr. Dan Moore. Dan feels that creating music is the art of sculpting sound. Whether in a live concert or the recording studio making art with sound is all about choosing the right tools to shape your music. All of the models in the Dan Moore line highlight his full-bodied, resonant, and “fruity” sound. These mallets work well on both marimba and vibes. You can mix and match to help create your own sculpture in sound. All models have rattan handles.

The DM21 medium vibraphone/marimba mallet features a full, warm, resonant tone with a tight, focused attack for solo marimba and vibes. The “Bel-Canto”sound provides an excellent timbre on extended range vibes or can be used for a soft vibe mallet in a small combo.

Name: Promark SPYR Medium Marimba Mallets
Description:
The Promark SPYR Medium Marimba Mallets perform across the entire range of the instrument. This creates a mallet that functions in any voice of a graduated set or general use. A slightly thicker shaft than traditional marimba mallets offers improved balance and feel.

The Promark SPYR Series uses a proprietary wrapping technique to offer a mallet line that delivers consistency, durability, great tone and projection in every mallet. Consistency like this creates a mallet perfect for high school or college level directors looking to outfit their sections with quality implements for any situation.

Name: Malletech Chamber Series CH18 Medium Hard Birch Marimba Mallets
Description:
The Malletech Chamber Series CH18 Medium Hard Birch Marimba Mallets are designed for maximum assertiveness on the instrument. Produces dynamic articulation and projection.

-Birch Handles.

Name: Innovative Casey Cangelosi CGL2Y Rich Articulate Marimba Mallets
Description:
Wrapped in a premium selected yarn, the Casey Cangelosi Series Marimba Mallets combines the rich, warm tonal characteristics of yarn with the articulate clarity of cord. These mallets are built on birch dowels with a natural finish and allow for a heavy bass sound without sounding muddy or washy, a delicate touch for lyrical legato passages that speak from the mallet immediately, and a steady timbre throughout the dynamic range.

The CGL2Y is intended to be used as a fast bass mallet and produces a rich bass sound that is less articulate than the CGL2.

Name: Innovative William Moersch IP512 - Medium Soft Marimba Mallets
Description:
This William Moersch IP512 Medium Soft Marimba Mallet has been designed exclusively for the marimba virtuoso. The rubber core offers a darker sound than the synthetic core models.

Features:
-rubber cores to create a full, dark tone and rich fundamental
-100% imported wool yarn
-Polished, hand-selected birch handles

Name: Marimba One Lynn Vartan Graduated Marimba Mallet Set - Rattan
Description:
The Marimba One Lynn Vartan series mallets are "just right" for any grip, any piece, any ensemble, or any venue. This graduated set includes one each of mallet LVR1, LVR3, LVR3, LVR5. Rattan handles.

The incredibly durable and warm-sounding yarns give clarity, articulation and depth. This versatile series covers your needs from the combo vibe/marimba mallets at the hard end of the range down to the super warm and rich soft mallet. You're sure to find your "just right" fit in this line to add to your quiver!

Name: Innovative Dan Moore DM26 Medium Hard Marimba/Vibe Mallet
Description:
This series of marimba and vibraphone mallets was developed for musician, composer and educator Dr. Dan Moore. Dan feels that creating music is the art of sculpting sound. Whether in a live concert or the recording studio making art with sound is all about choosing the right tools to shape your music. All of the models in the Dan Moore line highlight his full-bodied, resonant, and “fruity” sound. These mallets work well on both marimba and vibes. You can mix and match to help create your own sculpture in sound. All models have rattan handles.

The DM26 medium hard marimba/vibraphone mallet has a little more presence but still offers the same full-bodied marimba sound that is hallmark of the DM series. It offers slightly more projetion for solor and duo marimba. solo vibraphone and chamber music. The DM26 has a great sound on the full range of a 5.0 octave marimba and 3.5 octave vibraphone.

Name: Encore TM2 "Two Mallet" General Rattan Marimba Mallets
Description: The TM2 general mallets are part of the newest series offered from Encore Mallets. With most marimba mallets today being designed for four mallet playing, Bill Patterson, percussionist with the San Antonio Symphony, and Dan Lidster (founder of Encore Mallets) have developed two models of mallets specifically designed for two mallet playing. They have a thin rattan handle with a small weighted yarn covered head. These work well for state level competitions as well as professional playing. The TM2 is also a great choice for the Creston Marimba Concerto.

Name: Balter Ensemble Series12B Medium Hard Green Yarn Mallets - Birch
Description:
The Balter Ensemble Series Birch 12B Med. Hard Green Yarn are single-tone yarn wound mallets. Ideal weight for general playing.

Type: Green Yarn
Handle: Birch
Hardness: Medium Hard
Use: Marimba, Vibes

Name: Innovative She-e Wu WU5 Hard Birch Marimba Mallets
Description:
The She-e Wu Signature Series Marimba Mallets from Innovative Percussion feature seven mallets of varying hardness, with natural birch handles and a custom blended yarn wrap. Each captures the unique style, sensitivity and essence of the artist that is She-e Wu.

Features:
A combination of hard acrylic-style and hard rubber cores to create a full, bright tone and rich fundamental
Hand-selected unfinished natural birch handles
Wool-blend yarn wrap
Elongated shaped mallet heads

Name: Dragonfly Percussion Marimba Mallets Medium Hard with Birch Handles
Description: The Dragonfly Percussion Medium Hard marimba mallet is the most versatile of the series. Sounds and feels great throughout the entire range of a five octave marimba. The M4 is lighter and more maneuverable than the softer mallets in the series. Birch handle provide firm stability for the player.

Name: Yamaha Keiko Abe Signature Birch Keyboard Mallets Soft
Description:
The Soft Mallet from the Yamaha Keiko Abe Mallet Series is the MKAW07. The rubber core is wrapped with a 1mm tube is covered in a high quality yarn. The Birch shaft measures in 430mm and will feel extremely well balanced.

This mallet will be perfect for the lower octaves of a graduated set but could also work in some creative sets where the softer voice is the soprano or alto.

Name: Liberty One Scholastic Series Soft Marimba Mallets
Description:
The Liberty One Scholastic Series of Marimba Mallets come in three different head types for all of your playing situations and are Great Mallets at a Great Price.

This version is the LMS model which features a Soft Yarn playing head and is suitable for softer playing as well as on the lower octaves of the Marimba. This model can also be used for suspended cymbal passages that require a softer dynamic level while still achieving a great "whoosh" sound.

Handles - Birch
Soft Yarn

Name: Musser Good Vibes Mallets - Hard (M237)
Description:
Musser Good Vibes mallets have long been sought after and recognized as the world’s finest professional mallets. Handles are choice light rattan carefully selected and matched for size, weight, and flex. Select premium cord is wound around a solid, specially formulated and shaped rubber core, then carefully stitched.

Rattan Handle
Hardness - Hard
Cord Wrapped

Name: Yamaha Keiko Abe MKA-08 Very Soft Rattan Marimba Mallets
Description:
Yamaha's Keiko Abe Mallet line gives us the Very Soft Mallet from the collection. This mallet has a rubber core with a 2mm rubber wrapped around the core. Extra soft high quality yarn covers this core. The 430mm straight rattan shaft is selected to provide excellent balance for ease of performance.

The MKA08 will be a great bass or tenor mallet in a graduated set. Or it will serve on the real delicate parts where a light touch is required.

Name: Yamaha Keiko Abe MKA-01 Two-Tone Rattan Marimba Mallets
Description: This Is The MKA01 Mallet From Yamaha's Keiko Abe Series. This model feature s a dual core wrapped in synthetic leather. The Mallet is finished wrapped with high quality yarn. A 430mm Rattan shaft makes for a very well balanced, flexible Two Tone Mallet.

Name: Salyers Performance Collection Birch Marimba Mallets - Medium Hard
Description:
The Salyers Percussion PCM35 Performer Collection medium marimba mallet is great for when you need a little more precise attack. It works great on the upper half of the marimba. The mallets feature a dual surface birch shaft consisting of a natural, unlacquered grip area for maximum control. The upper portion of the shaft portion of the shaft is lacquered for strength and durability. The rubber and latex core is wrapped with a 100% wool yarn.

The Performance Collection marimba mallets were designed with the serious soloist in mind. The collection features rubber and synthetic cores with varying layers of latex and a wool yarn wrap for a warm full-bodied sound.

Name: Promark EG3 Evelyn Glennie Medium Birch Keyboard Mallets
Description:
Evelyn Glennie, O.B.E., is a Grammy Award-winning, internationally respected solo percussionist. She has performed with most of the world's famous symphony orchestras.
This mallet is designed for marimba or vibraphone. Cord wrapped, birch shaft.

Length: 16 1/2"

Name: Malletech Grand Soloist GS7 Medium Soft Birch Marimba Mallets
Description:
A medium-soft, but articulate, round-headed mallet that produces a full tone in all ranges. Single-toned, on birch shafts.

The Grand Soloist mallets have a rubber core plus an additional layer of latex, mounted on select birch shafts. Unlike most of Malletechs marimba mallets, they are similarly weighted. They are also wrapped differently than any of the other Malletech marimba models. The synthetic blend of yarn is wrapped medium-tightly and angled to produce a rounder head shape. Gordons design is a great choice for players who emphasize clear articulation.

Name: Malletech Chamber Series CH4 Soft Birch Marimba Mallets
Description:
The Malletech Chamber Series CH4 Soft Birch Marimba Mallets deliver smooth rolling, but robust bass.

-Birch Handles

Name: Marimba One Wave Wrap WWXR2 Medium Hard Rattan Mallets
Description: The WWXR2 is a medium hard mallet with Gray/Sangria colored yarn and rattan handles. The highest concentration of yarn is wrapped around the mallet's equator, creating an increased striking surface and a mallet that is more responsive than traditionally wrapped mallets. The Wave Wrap's blend of wool and nylon yarn minimizes contact sound and enhances articulation. Using Wave Wrap technology, the mallet is balanced to facilitate the ease of movement in the player's hand. The double equatorial wrap creates a chevron pattern around the playing surface and a distinctive wave on top of the head - two unique visual signatures for these finest of marimba mallets.

Name: Innovative Artisan Series - Multi-tone Marimba Mallet - Cedar
Description:
The Innovative Multi-Tone Marimba Mallets allow the player to achieve different levels of articulation without changing mallets. Ideal for performances that feature drastic, sudden dynamic changes.

Multi-tone graduated rubber yarn wrapped core
Cedar handles

Name: Malletech Grand Soloist Graduated Marimba Mallet Set - Hard
Description:
The Malletech Grand Soloist Hard Graduated Marimba Mallet Set set consists of one GS7, 2 GS13’s and one GS16.

Sold as a set of 4.

Name: Innovative Artisan Series - Medium Soft Marimba Mallet - Cedar
Description:
The Innovative Artisan Series Medium Soft Marimba Mallet functions across the entire instrument delivering full tonalities with a medium soft attack. Perfect for softer passages that span the entire instrument or a baritone voice in a graduated set.

Medium Soft graduated rubber core
Cedar handles

Name: Promark SPYR Medium-Hard Marimba Mallets
Description:
The Promark SPYR Medium-Hard Marimba Mallets function across the entire instrument delivering rich fundamentals with a slightly increased attack over the medium mallets. These perform in any voice in a graduated set. A slightly thicker shaft than traditional marimba mallets offers improved balance and feel.

The Promark SPYR Series uses a proprietary wrapping technique to offer a mallet line that delivers consistency, durability, great tone and projection in every mallet. Consistency like this creates a mallet perfect for high school or college level directors looking to outfit their sections with quality implements for any situation.

Name: Musser Good Vibes Mallets - Medium Soft (M233)
Description:
Musser Good Vibes mallets have long been sought after and recognized as the world’s finest professional mallets. Handles are choice light rattan carefully selected and matched for size, weight, and flex. Select premium cord is wound around a solid, specially formulated and shaped rubber core, then carefully stitched.

Rattan Handle
Hardness - Medium Soft
Cord Wrapped

Name: Innovative Soloist Series IP300N Med Hard Birch Marimba Mallets - Natural Handles
Description:
Innovative Percussion's most popular series of solo marimba mallets is now offered with unfinished birch handles thanks to countless requests. All six models are available with a natural finish that provides a more slip resistant grip that is ideal for players who prefer an enhanced grip on their mallets.

The Innovative Percussion IP300N Soloist Series Medium Hard Marimba Mallets are slightly harder than the IP275 model and are designed for use in the upper 4 octaves of a 5 octave marimba. This model features 100% wool yarn and hard rubber cores that help to create a full, rich tome and warm fundamental.

Designed with the marimba virtuoso in mind, the Soloist Series is constructed using hard rubber cores, covered in a series of latex combinations. The mallets are wrapped is a 100% wool wrap, custom manufactured specifically for Innovative Percussion, which delivers a brilliant, rich tone with the warmest fundamental possible in all registers of the marimba.

Name: Balter Ensemble Series 14B Medium Soft Red Yarn Mallets - Birch
Description:
The Balter Ensemble Series Birch 14B Med. Soft Red Yarn is one of the cornerstones of the Mike Balter keyboard mallet line. These single-tone yarn wound mallets feature solid rubber cores that are the ideal weight for general playing. Their proven reliability has made them an industry standard that continues to exceed the needs of the player.

Type: Red Yarn
Handle: Birch
Hardness: Medium Soft
Use: Marimba, Vibes

Name: Encore Adam Tan Series Marimba Mallets with Birch Handles - Medium
Description:
The Encore Adam Tan Series Marimba Mallets offer a medium weight feel designed for concert solo performances, bringing out the warmth and fundamentals of your marimba sound. The AT Series is comfortable in the hands and plays easily, ideal for all levels and styles of marimba performance.

The mallets feature a latex-wrapped, thick, rubber disk core covered in all natural North American Alpaca wool. The series is also slightly two-toned and extremely versatile in all applications.

AT3 (Medium, Purple) – The perfect general mallet. For warm, clear sound in all registers of the marimba. Works well as any voice in a graduated set. Great for all solo situations for projection and clarity while remaining rounded and not hard sounding.

Name: Innovative Rattan Series RS201 Soft Rattan Vibe/Marimba Mallets
Description: This line of mallets has been designed for the contemporary vibraphonist. The rubber mushroom shaped core achieves a rich and fundamentally warm tone. Each pair is hand matched for weight, size and sound.

Name: Vic Firth Anders Astrand Orange Series M292 Medium Keyboard Mallets
Description:
The Vic Firth Anders Astrand Orange Series M292 Medium Keyboard Mallets perform across the entire range of the marimba and vibraphone. This model is the most versatile of the series delivering a wide range of dynamics.

The mallets in the Vic Firth Anders Astrand Orange Series each possess their own unique character as well as sharing a common sound quality across the series. This creates a mallet that performs well in an ensemble where the tones need to blend and compliment each other. The Orange Series functions as a solo mallet as well.

Head - Cord
Handles - Rattan

Name: Innovative Fundamental Series F8.5 Hard Rubber Marimba Mallets
Description:
Innovative Percussion F8.5 Hard Rubber Marimba Mallets feature black painted birch handles.

Manufactured by the leader in high quality keyboard mallets, the Innovative Percussion Fundamental Series Keyboard Mallets were specifically designed with the beginning percussionist and budget conscious band director in mind. This economical series offers the beginning percussionist and band director a line of quality keyboard mallets at an affordable price.

The Fundamental Series is available in 15 different models. All models feature black birch dowels and the two vibraphone models are also available with high quality rattan handles.

Name: Vic Firth Terry Gibbs Keyboard - Hard - Red Cord
Description:
Vic Firth M33 Terry Gibbs Vibraphone or Marimba Mallets were conceived through extensive discussion and research with Terry Gibbs. Hard vibe or marimba. Rattan handles.

Length = 15 1/4"

Name: Vic Firth Rosauro Keyboard Mallets - Hybrid General
Description:
Vic Firth M228 Ney Rosauro General Keyboard Mallets are an all purpose mallet for rich, full sound on any keyboard.

Ney Rosauro designed this special series of marimba and vibraphone mallets to reflect the sound he desires for his award winning compositions. Rattan shafts and rubber cores coupled with special yarn and cord choices assure the player of a pure and natural sound that projects the full capabilities of the instrument.

Length: 16 1/4"

Name: Innovative Artisan Series - Medium Hard Marimba Mallet - Rattan
Description:
The Innovative Artisan Series Medium Hard Marimba Mallet functions across the entire instrument delivering fast articulate tonalities. The medium hardness of this mallet makes this perfect when the performance requires fast and defined lower registrar notes.

Medium hard graduated rubber core
Rattan handles

Name: Malletech Escape Ten EX10 Marimba Mallets
Description: The Malletech Escape Ten EX10 Marimba Mallets are medium-heavy yarn mallets that provide a focused, warm sound with clear articulation across the entire instrument.

Name: Marimba One Ivana Bilic IBB2 Medium Hard Birch Mallets
Description:
The Marimba One Ivana Bilic model IBB2 Singing Tenor mallets are an expressive solo singing mallet, with a clear but warm and noble sound. It is ideal as a general mallet, adaptive to individual playing style and wide repertoire.

Ivana Bilic wanted her new mallet line to emphasize the singing characteristic of the marimba, by relating the sound that each mallet makes to human vocal qualities. Her definitions for each mallet combine the ideal playing range for a mallet with the color/timbre of its sound.

These mallets are on Birch Handles.

Name: Marimba One Double Helix DHR5 Medium Soft Rattan Mallets
Description:
The Double Helix Series was developed to bring out the warmth of the fundamental throughout the range of the 5-octave marimba. The relaxed yarn tension on these mallets enhances the smooth, dark attack of the mallet.

The exclusive patent pending “double helix wrap” technique allows you to easily identify each mallet in the series. Two strands of yarn are wrapped independently of each other, creating a woven effect. After one of the strands makes a revolution around the mallet head, the other strand makes a revolution, securing the previous strand. This locks the yarn in place and prevents it from unraveling. This unique method of wrapping provides our mallets with their distinct look, and exceptional sound.

Name: Malletech Leigh Howard Stevens LS20 Medium–Very Hard Birch Marimba Mallets
Description: Medium to very hard, blue label, and multi-toned. This is a general use mallet that is medium to very hard. The LS20 is articulate and bright in tone above mezzo forte. Birch shafts.

Name: Innovative Casey Cangelosi CGL1 Heavy Bass Marimba Mallets
Description:
Wrapped in an environmentally friendly bamboo-blended cord, the Casey Cangelosi Series Marimba Mallets combines the rich, warm tonal characteristics of yarn with the articulate clarity of cord. These mallets are built on birch dowels with a natural finish and allow for a heavy bass sound without sounding muddy or washy, a delicate touch for lyrical legato passages that speak from the mallet immediately, and a steady timbre throughout the dynamic range. The two softest mallets are also available with a soft yarn wrap.

The CGL1 is a heavy mallet, producing a thick, articulate bass sound.

Name: Innovative William Moersch IP504 Hard Marimba Mallets
Description:
This four model series of mallets has been designed exclusively for marimba virtuoso William Moersch. The hard acrylic-style core offers a full sound at soft dynamic levels and a bright sound at louder dynamic levels.

Features:
-Hard acrylic-style cores to create a full, bright tone and rich fundamental
-100% imported wool yarn
-Polished, hand-selected birch handles

Name: Encore NZ2R Nancy Zeltsman Medium Harder Rattan Marimba Mallets
Description:
The Encore NZ2R Nancy Zeltsman "Medium Harder" mallets speak very easily, and produce a clear, warmer sound-perfect for playing a melody with the upper mallet. These are intended for more general use than the NZ1s. Rattan Handles.

One unique characteristic of my line is that the oval-shape of the mallet head is horizontal rather than vertical. I imagine (and maybe it's true) that the width of the mallet activates more of the vertical grains of wood in every bar-thus contributing to a richer tone. Everything about the construction of my line of mallets-from the cores, to the latex, to the type of yarn-is aimed at helping you to produce a fundamental-rich tone.

- Nancy Zeltsman

Hardness - Medium Harder
Handles - Rattan

Name: Encore NM6B Nanae Mimura Soft Birch Marimba Mallets
Description: Nanae Mimura Series mallets are slightly heavier than NZ Series mallets. They feature rubber disk cores hand wrapped with latex, and are covered by North American wool. Encore's unique hand wrapped latex process enhances the fundamental allowing harder mallets which do not sound harsh, and improves the feel of the mallet's rebound. The yarn is all natural wool which is not chemically processed and therefore particularly soft and durable. (Our yarn is purchased from the sheep farm that harvests the wool - the yarn is so natural that you may occasionally see a small piece of hay from a mallet head!) The softness of their yarn combined with the hardness and clarity of their latex-wrapped disk cores make NM Series mallets two-toned and very versatile. Soft, with birch shafts.

Name: Innovative Eriko Daimo Series Medium Marimba Mallets - Yarn Wrapped
Description:
This Innovative Eriko Daimo Series Marimba Mallet features a tightly wrapped rubber core with a custom blended yarn wrap. This model is intended for general all purpose playing.

Handles - rattan
Hardness- Medium
Length 17"
Red tape indicator on handle

Name: Encore NM5B Nanae Mimura Medium Soft Birch Marimba Mallets
Description: Nanae Mimura Series mallets are slightly heavier than NZ Series mallets. They feature rubber disk cores hand wrapped with latex, and are covered by North American wool. Encore's unique hand wrapped latex process enhances the fundamental allowing harder mallets which do not sound harsh, and improves the feel of the mallet's rebound. The yarn is all natural wool which is not chemically processed and therefore particularly soft and durable. (Our yarn is purchased from the sheep farm that harvests the wool - the yarn is so natural that you may occasionally see a small piece of hay from a mallet head!) The softness of their yarn combined with the hardness and clarity of their latex-wrapped disk cores make NM Series mallets two-toned and very versatile. Medium soft, with birch shafts.

Name: Vic Firth Theodor Milkov Medium Hard Marimba Mallets
Description:
The Vic Firth Theodor Milkov Medium Hard Marimba Mallets are the general mallets of the series. This means they function across the entire instrument delivering full and clear tonalities.

Length: 17''
Head material/color: Mohair Yarn/Black
Handle Material: Hickory

Name: Innovative She-e Wu WU1 Soft Birch Marimba Mallets
Description:
The She-e Wu Signature Series Marimba Mallets from Innovative Percussion feature seven mallets of varying hardness, with natural birch handles and a custom blended yarn wrap. Each captures the unique style, sensitivity and essence of the artist that is She-e Wu.

Features:
A combination of hard acrylic-style and hard rubber cores to create a full, bright tone and rich fundamental
Hand-selected unfinished natural birch handles
Wool-blend yarn wrap
Elongated shaped mallet heads

Name: Encore NM2B Nanae Mimura Hard Birch Marimba Mallets
Description: Nanae Mimura Series mallets are slightly heavier than NZ Series mallets. They feature rubber disk cores hand wrapped with latex, and are covered by North American wool. Encore's unique hand wrapped latex process enhances the fundamental allowing harder mallets which do not sound harsh, and improves the feel of the mallet's rebound. The yarn is all natural wool which is not chemically processed and therefore particularly soft and durable. (Our yarn is purchased from the sheep farm that harvests the wool - the yarn is so natural that you may occasionally see a small piece of hay from a mallet head!) The softness of their yarn combined with the hardness and clarity of their latex-wrapped disk cores make NM Series mallets two-toned and very versatile. Medium harder, with birch shafts.

Name: Musser Good Vibes Mallets - Medium Hard (M229)
Description:
Musser Good Vibes mallets have long been sought after and recognized as the world’s finest professional mallets. Handles are choice light rattan carefully selected and matched for size, weight, and flex. Select premium cord is wound around a solid, specially formulated and shaped rubber core, then carefully stitched.

Rattan Handle
Hardness - Medium Hard
Cord Wrapped

Name: Marimba One Katarzyna Mycka KMB1 Very Hard Birch Mallets
Description:
The Katarzyna Mycka Professional Mallet Series are wrapped very tightly with all synthetic yarn. The combination of synthetic yarn and the tight wrap gives the immediate response of the bar that is required for ensemble and orchestra playing. The heavy weight of these mallets brings out the warmth of the bars by physically putting into motion the fundamental frequency of the bars.

Katarzyna recommends using this very hard mallet for such pieces as Eckhard Kopetzki's Etude Hommage, Concerto for Marimba and Strings (4th Mvmt).

Name: Innovative William Moersch IP514 - Hard Marimba Mallets
Description:
This William Moersch IP514 Hard Marimba Mallet has been designed exclusively for the marimba virtuoso. The rubber core offers a darker sound than the synthetic core models.

Features:
-rubber cores to create a full, dark tone and rich fundamental
-100% imported wool yarn
-Polished, hand-selected birch handles

Name: Innovative Eriko Daimo Series Medium Hard Marimba/Vibraphone Mallets - Cord Wrapped
Description:
This Innovative Eriko Daimo Series Marimba / Vibraphone Mallet features a tightly wrapped rubber core with a durable synthetic cord. This model is intended for precise articulate playing.

Handles - rattan
Hardness- Medium Hard
Length 16.625"
White tape indicator on handle

Name: Yamaha Keiko Abe MKA-S7 Very Soft Rattan Marimba Mallets
Description: Designed in close collaboration with Keiko Abe, her Signature Series Mallets are designed to draw out the most from both the performer's technique and tone from the instrument in compositions that require exceptional technique. Ideal for advanced players, these MKAS7 mallets are much heavier than regular mallets. This causes the marimba's tone bars to resonate fully creating a sound that has been up until now, difficult to obtain. The round yarn wound heads are heavier than standard mallets, but they are fully capable of delivering delicate expression. Rattan handles.

Name: Innovative Weichen Lin Marimba Mallets Birch - Bass
Description:
The Innovative Weichen Lin Bass Marimba Mallets function perfectly on the bass notes on the instrument allowing you to achieve punchy bass note to smooth rolls. The blended yarn creates an incredibly versatile mallet, capable of lush Bach chorales as well as more contemporary articulate passages. Built on birch shafts, the rubber cores provide a strong fundamental tone with very little contact sound.

-16 3/4" Total Length

Name: Innovative Artisan Series - Hard Marimba Mallet - Cedar
Description:
The Innovative Artisan Series Hard Marimba Mallet delivers fast articulate tones with a balanced natural feel. This mallet functions as either the soprano or tenor voice in a graduated set.

Hard graduated rubber core
Cedar handle

Name: Innovative Ivan Trevino Bass Marimba Mallets
Description:
The Innovative Ivan Trevino Bass Marimba mallets features a core tailored for a distinct sound and balance. This mallet functions on the lower octaves delivering well rounded bass tones with clean a clean fundamental. The marimba models feature longer shafts for ease of playing wide intervals.

Handles - Walnut stained birch
Synthetic Core
Dark Blue tape indicator on the handle
Length - 16.75"

Name: Innovative Eriko Daimo Series Medium Soft Marimba Mallets - Yarn Wrapped
Description:
This Innovative Eriko Daimo Series Marimba Mallet features a tightly wrapped rubber core with a custom blended yarn wrap. This model is intended for General all purpose playing when a little less attack is required.

Handles - rattan
Hardness- Medium Soft
Length 17"
Purple tape indicator on handle

Name: Encore 32AYR Yarn Wound Series General Rattan Mallets
Description: Introduced in 1982, the Yarn Series mallets were the first latex-wrapped core mallets in history, and have since remained staple implements of percussionists the world over. These are the mallets that set Encore Mallets, Inc. apart from the rest of the industry. Their full, rich, and round sound of maximum fundamental and minimum attack is achieved through the skillful combination of hand-wrapped latex and durable 100% sport wool yarn. For that classic Encore sound, the YR Series is place to start and stay. Medium, with rattan shafts.

Name: Malletech Leigh Howard Stevens LS15L Medium Soft-Hard Birch Marimba Mallets
Description: Medium soft to hard, red label, and multi-toned. A full range medium soft to hard mallet that speaks clearly in all registers, yet still rolls smoothly. The most popular “general use” mallet in the Stevens Series. The LS15L model is the original, lighter LS15.

Name: Balter Basics Series Birch BB3 Soft Marimba Mallets Red Yarn
Description:
This Soft Marimba Mallet is from the Balter Basics Series. The softness of the mallet head is for lighter, softer passages or developing technique. The Balter basics Series is a great place to start a student off into the world of keyboard percussion.

Soft mallet head covered in red yarn
red colored birch mallet handle

Name: Schlagkraft Zivkovic Marimba Mallets - Blue Strong General (Cedar)
Description:
The Schlagkraft Zivkovic Blue Strong General is a perfect model for overall performance, it covers entire range of the marimba. It performs very well in low register as well as the highest octaves. An ideal mallet for pieces that cover the entire range of the instrument.

The Zivkovich Series Mallets feature rubber cores wrapped in layers of a synthetic yarn. Schlagkraft hand selects and matches the cedar shafts to create a balanced even pair.

Cedar Handles
Medium

Name: Innovative Percussion Sandi Rennick Series Medium Hard Marimba Mallets - Sage Green Cord
Description:
The IP4000C series features cord-wound marimba mallets designed to the specifications of Sandi Rennick, the Front Ensemble Arranger of the 9-time DCI World Champion Santa Clara Vanguard. Known for putting more weight in the players hands, these models produce a focused sound which speaks quickly, and was created with today's modern amplified keyboard section in mind. The addition of a cord-wrapped option adds enhanced articulation and a new voice for the front ensemble.

Starting with durable birch handles in a matte finish, these implements are wrapped with cord allowing them to be articulate and cutting while withstanding the needs of rigorous rehearsal and performance schedules. The versatility found in the Sandi Rennick series makes balancing and blending your ensemble easier than ever before.

Sandi Rennick Series
IP4001: Soft Marimba
IP4001C: Soft Marimba - Cord
IP4002: Medium Marimba
IP4002C: Medium Marimba - Cord
IP4002.5: Medium Hard Marimba
IP4002.5C: Medium Hard Marimba - Cord
IP4003: Hard Marimba
IP4003C: Hard Marimba - Cord
IP4004: Wrapped Xylophone
IP4005: Soft Vibraphone
IP4006: Medium Vibraphone
IP4007: Hard Vibraphone

Name: Innovative Rattan Series RS20 Medium Rattan Marimba/Vibraphone Mallet
Description:
The Innovative Percussion RS20 medium marimba/vibraphone mallets are constructed on durable rattan handles with large
oval cores and are tightly wrapped with synthetic yarn.

Name: Dragonfly Percussion Marimba Mallets Medium Hard with Rattan Handles
Description: The Most Versatile of Dragonfly's Marimba Mallet Series is the Medium Hard Mallet. This Mallet feels and sounds great across the entire five octaves of a marimba. Being lighter than its softer counterparts it provides great maneuverability. Functions great in more than one roll in a graduated set.

Name: Yamaha Keiko Abe Signature Birch Keyboard Mallets Hard (MKAW-04)
Description:
The MKAW04 from the Yamaha Keiko Abe Mallet Series is also a hard mallet but lighter in feel than the MKA03. The MKAW04 features a rubber core wrapped with a 1mm rubber tube. The Birch Shaft on the MKA04 is 430mm and the mallet is finished with a high quality yarn.

This mallet will excel as a soprano mallet in a graduated set. Perfect for the upper octaves of the marimba.

Name: Marimba One Conrado Moya Birch Marimba Mallets - Medium
Description: The Marimba One Conrado Moya Medium Birch Mallets deliver a clear fundamental across the entire range of the instrument. Each mallet features a heavy weighted core with tight tensioned yarn. The Moya mallets they achieve clear articulation with a very warm texture, even with the hardest mallets. Designed specifically played with big orchestras, choirs, and percussion ensembles they are also delicate enough to be used in the most intimate chamber music and solo works.

Name: Salyers Performance Collection Rattan Mallets - Medium Rubber
Description:
The Salyers Percussion PCX42 medium rubber mallet works well on marimba or xylophone as well as woodblocks. The rattan shafts are matched in diameter and flex.

The Performance Collection unwound mallets are designed to offer a comprehensive selection of sonic possibilities to the advanced percussionist. Head sizes available are 1″, 1.125″ and 1.25″. The rattan size sets this series apart from any other series available. The trend of using very thick rattan on unwound mallets results in a stiff shaft that does not allow the natural flex of rattan to help get the best sound from the keys. We use a rattan size that feels good yet flexes just enough to help you get the best sound possible from your instruments.

Name: Malletech Michael Burritt Ensemble Series Marimba Mallets - Medium Soft MBE7
Description:
Malletech Michael Burritt Ensemble Series Medium Soft Marimba Mallets offer a warm, versatile and articulate sound. Perfect for low to mid range of the instrument.

Being one of the softer mallets of the series, these offer rich, dark, and round lows while still being remarkably focused and clear. The weighting of the mallets have a nice ‘throw’ that allows you to play though the bar with ease, resulting in a bigger sound with less effort.

The Michael Burritt Ensemble Series is an incredibly versatile series designed to excel in musical settings ranging from solo, to chamber, and ensembles of all sizes. The mallets deliver a warm, woody tone that is robust and round while still providing the strong articulation required to cut through the ensemble.

Synthetic yarn
Medium Soft Hardness
Birch Shafts
Acorn Shaped Mallet Head

Name: Marimba One Colin Currie CCB3 Medium Soft Birch Mallets
Description: The Colin Currie Signature Mallet Series was created as a simple and uncomplicated series of mallets. The series contains four mallets featuring one unwrapped mallet and three wrapped mallets. They allow spontaneity, which can be used with as much force or delicacy without the unsatisfactory change in timbre this approach can create. These mallets represent a focused, polished approach to sound.

Name: Encore Adam Series Marimba Mallets - Graduated Set Rattan
Description:
The Encore Adam Tan Series Marimba Mallets offer a medium weight feel designed for concert solo performances, bringing out the warmth and fundamentals of your marimba sound. The AT Series is comfortable in the hands and plays easily, ideal for all levels and styles of marimba performance.

The mallets feature a latex-wrapped, thick, rubber disk core covered in all natural North American Alpaca wool. The series is also slightly two-toned and extremely versatile in all applications.

The Adam Tan Series Graduated Set is a great starting point for a warm yet clear graduated set with slight weight, that can be used for all kinds of solo repertoire and on all sizes and materials of marimba.

Featuring (from left to right) the AT4 (Medium Soft), two AT3s (Medium) and an AT2 (Medium Hard). You have versatile bass, mid, and high range mallet coverage in addition to being able to use the two AT3s for two mallet solo and ensemble playing when needed.

Name: Vic Firth Rosauro Keyboard Mallets - Medium Soft Marimba
Description:
Vic Firth M222 Ney Rosauro Medium Soft Marimba Mallets produce a warm and round sound while maintaining clear articulation. With rattan shafts.

Ney Rosauro designed this special series of marimba mallets to reflect the sound he desires for his award winning compositions. Rattan shafts and rubber cores coupled with special yarn and cord choices assure the player of a pure and natural sound that projects the full capabilities of the instrument.

Length: 16 3/4"

Name: Malletech Grand Soloist Graduated Marimba Mallet Set - Soft
Description:
The Malletech Grand Soloist Soft Graduated Marimba Mallet Set set consists of one GS3, 2 GS7’s and one GS13.

Sold as a set of 4.

Name: Salyers Performance Collection Rattan Mallets - 1.25" Hytrel
Description:
The Salyers Percussion PCX21 features a large 1.25″ soft plastic ball. The rubber-like material works extremely well on marimba, vibraphone and xylophone. The rattan shafts are matched in diameter and flex. This is a very versatile mallet.

The Performance Collection unwound mallets are designed to offer a comprehensive selection of sonic possibilities to the advanced percussionist. Head sizes available are 1″, 1.125″ and 1.25″. The rattan size sets this series apart from any other series available. The trend of using very thick rattan on unwound mallets results in a stiff shaft that does not allow the natural flex of rattan to help get the best sound from the keys. We use a rattan size that feels good yet flexes just enough to help you get the best sound possible from your instruments.

Name: Malletech Michael Burritt Ensemble Series Marimba Mallets - Hard MBE17
Description:
Malletech Michael Burritt Ensemble Series Hard Marimba Mallets offer a cutting sound with bite yet not harsh. Perfect for mid to high range of the instrument.

Being one of the harder mallets of the series, these offer clarity and transparency with a bit of bite. The birch handles and weighting of the mallets have a nice ‘throw’ that allows you to play though the bar with ease, resulting in a bigger sound with less effort.

The Michael Burritt Ensemble Series is an incredibly versatile series designed to excel in musical settings ranging from solo, to chamber, and ensembles of all sizes. The mallets deliver a warm, woody tone that is robust and round while still providing the strong articulation required to cut through the ensemble.

Synthetic Yarn
Hard Hardness
Birch Shafts
Acorn Shaped Mallet Head

Name: Marimba One Wave Wrap WWXR4 Medium Soft Rattan Mallets
Description: The WWXR4 is a medium soft mallet with Gray/Cinnamon colored yarn and rattan handles. The highest concentration of yarn is wrapped around the mallet's equator, creating an increased striking surface and a mallet that is more responsive than traditionally wrapped mallets. The Wave Wrap's blend of wool and nylon yarn minimizes contact sound and enhances articulation. Using Wave Wrap technology, the mallet is balanced to facilitate the ease of movement in the player's hand. The double equatorial wrap creates a chevron pattern around the playing surface and a distinctive wave on top of the head - two unique visual signatures for these finest of marimba mallets.

Name: Malletech Escape Ten EX14 Marimba Mallets
Description: The Malletech Escape Ten EX14 Marimba Mallets are a medium-heavy hard yarn mallet that delivers a focused, warm sound. The delivered articulation is best suited in the mid and upper range of the instrument while being clearly heard in a musical texture with adjacent mallets.

Name: Malletech Blake Tyson BT9 Marimba Mallets - Soft to Medium Hard
Description:
The new line of Blake Tyson mallets offer great expressive potential. They respond to subtle changes of touch, and their depth of sound and sensitive response help to create an amazing variety of timbres and articulation. The Blake Tyson mallets are made with durable wood blend yarn on birch handles.

The BT9 provides full bass tones while maintaining a clear sound in the top register of the marimba. They are great for quick transitions between rolled and struck-note passages. Soft to medium hard.

Name: Marimba One Beverley Johnston Soft Marimba Mallets - Birch
Description:
The Marimba One Beverley Johnson Soft Marimba Mallets function perfectly on the lower registrars of the instrument delivering resonant definitive sound. This mallets utilizes the Wave Wrap Technology creating a balanced feel as well as allowing it to function on the Vibraphone as well. Like most Marimba One Mallets this series adheres to strict manufacturing standards functions across a wide musical range from the contemporary to the classical.

Hard Mallet Head
Birch Handles

Name: Innovative Mark Ford Rhapsody Series - IP823 Hard Marimba Mallets
Description:
Innovative Mark Ford Rhapsody Series Concerto Hard Marimba Mallets are designed to deliver a brighter more articulate sound than other Mark Ford Mallets. The Hard Mallet is perfect for upper octaves of the instrument delivering fast articulate tonalities. It functions well as the soprano roll in graduated set.

Hard mallet core
Birch handles

Name: Innovative Ludwig Albert IP3107 Hard Rattan Marimba Mallets
Description:
This line of professional keyboard mallets has been specifically designed for Belgium marimba artist Ludwig Albert III. The eight models feature shafts constructed of superior quality rattan and specially weighted rubber cores of varying sizes and degrees of hardness. Wool-blend yarn is used to fully maximize the dynamic potential of the line, while the unique shape allows for precise tonal clarity. The length provides an ultimate balance, which will meet the needs of today's professional player.

IP3101 Extra Soft Marimba
IP3102 Medium Extra Soft Marimba
IP3103 Soft Marimba
IP3104 Medium Soft Marimba
IP3105 Medium Marimba
IP3106 Medium Hard Marimba
IP3107 Hard Marimba
IP3108 Extra Hard Marimba

Rubber cores of varying size, shape, and hardness


Superior quality rattan


Wool-blend yarn wrap
