Description:
This line of professional keyboard mallets has been specifically designed for Belgium marimba artist Ludwig Albert III. The eight models feature shafts constructed of superior quality rattan and specially weighted rubber cores of varying sizes and degrees of hardness. Wool-blend yarn is used to fully maximize the dynamic potential of the line, while the unique shape allows for precise tonal clarity. The length provides an ultimate balance, which will meet the needs of today's professional player.

IP3108 Extra Hard Marimba

Superior quality rattan

Wool-blend yarn wrap

Name: Innovative Pius Cheung PIUS1 Soft Bass Marimba Mallets
Description:
The Innovative Percussion Pius Cheung Series Marimba Mallets features six models with rubber cores of varying hardness. Constructed on rattan handles, each model is wrapped with a very unique alpaca-blend yarn that allows the player to produce a tone of gold at extreme dynamic ranges from the utmost silky pianissimo to peasante fortissimo with minimal effort.

The PIUS1 is a soft, powerful bass mallet for solo playing.

Name: Innovative Eriko Daimo Series Bass Marimba Mallets - Yarn Wrapped
Description:
This Innovative Eriko Daimo Series Marimba Mallet features a tightly wrapped rubber core with a custom blended yarn wrap. This model is intended for The lower octaves of the instrument with a slight articulate attack on the bars.

Handles - rattan
Length 17"
Light blue tape indicator on handle

Name: Yamaha Keiko Abe Signature Birch Keyboard Mallets Two-Tone Head
Description: This Is The MKAW01 Mallet From Yamaha's Keiko Abe Series. This model feature s a dual core wrapped in synthetic leather. The Mallet is finished wrapped with high quality yarn. A 430mm Birch shaft makes for a very well balanced, flexible Two Tone Mallet.

Name: Marimba One Katarzyna Mycka KMB6 Soft Birch Mallets
Description:
The Katarzyna Mycka Professional Mallet Series are wrapped very tightly with all synthetic yarn. The combination of synthetic yarn and the tight wrap gives the immediate response of the bar that is required for ensemble and orchestra playing. The heavy weight of these mallets brings out the warmth of the bars by physically putting into motion the fundamental frequency of the bars.

Katarzyna recommends using this soft mallet for such pieces as David Maslanka's My Lady White (1st Mvmt) and Ney Rosauro's Concerto No. 1 (2nd Mvmt, left hand).

Name: Dragonfly Percussion Marimba Mallets Medium Soft with Birch Handles
Description: Dragonfly Percussion Medium soft marimba mallets give the player a soft touch with that little added attack and definition when needed. Suited for the lower octaves of a five octave marimba, this Mallet would be perfect as the tenor or bass mallet in a graduated set. The rubber core and cloth covering results in a lot of fundamental and clear attack. Birch handles provide stability players look for.

Name: Encore Adam Tan Series Marimba Mallets with Birch Handles - Medium Hard
Description:
The Encore Adam Tan Series Marimba Mallets offer a medium weight feel designed for concert solo performances, bringing out the warmth and fundamentals of your marimba sound. The AT Series is comfortable in the hands and plays easily, ideal for all levels and styles of marimba performance.

The mallets feature a latex-wrapped, thick, rubber disk core covered in all natural North American Alpaca wool. The series is also slightly two-toned and extremely versatile in all applications.

AT2 (Medium Hard, Violet) – For slightly warm, more articulate pronounced sound in a marimba’s middle to high registers. Works well as a top voice in a graduated set. Great for solo works where you need more clarity and projection.

Name: Yamaha Keiko Abe Signature Birch Keyboard Mallets Very Soft
Description:
Yamaha's Keiko Abe Mallet line gives us the Very Soft Mallet from the collection. This mallet has a rubber core with a 2mm rubber wrapped around the core. Extra soft high quality yarn covers this core. The 430mm straight Birch shaft is selected to provide excellent balance for ease of performance.

The MKAW08 will be a great bass or tenor mallet in a graduated set. Or it will serve on the real delicate parts where a light touch is required.

Name: Vic Firth American Custom Keyboard - Medium Head - Round
Description:
Vic Firth M2 American Custom Keyboard Mallets feature a medium hard round head for all-around playing. Vic Firth M2 mallets are 16" long.

The Vic Firth M2 American Custom Keyboard Mallets are designed to address a range of instruments and all dynamic levels. This yarn model features a 3-ply nylon yarn that is virtually indestructible yet provides excellent cushioning for a softer sound.

With birch shafts for rigidity and consistency, they are favored by players who prefer extra reach without the added flexibility of rattan.

Name: Innovative Rattan Series RS50C Very Hard Marimba/Vibraphone Mallets
Description:
This Innovative Rattan Series Hard Marimba/Vibraphone Mallet is designed for the percussion ensemble or contemporary mallet player. A tightly cord wrapped graduated rubber core allows this mallet to function on mallet instruments as well as other accessory percussion.
- Matched Rattan Handles

Name: Encore 33YR Yarn Wound Series Medium Soft Rattan Mallets
Description: Introduced in 1982, the Yarn Series mallets were the first latex-wrapped core mallets in history, and have since remained staple implements of percussionists the world over. These are the mallets that set Encore Mallets, Inc. apart from the rest of the industry. Their full, rich, and round sound of maximum fundamental and minimum attack is achieved through the skillful combination of hand-wrapped latex and durable 100% sport wool yarn. For that classic Encore sound, the YR Series is place to start and stay. Medium soft, with rattan shafts.

Name: Innovative William Moersch IP501 Soft Marimba Mallets
Description:
This four model series of mallets has been designed exclusively for marimba virtuoso William Moersch. The hard acrylic-style core offers a full sound at soft dynamic levels and a bright sound at louder dynamic levels.

Features:
-Hard acrylic-style cores to create a full, bright tone and rich fundamental
-100% imported wool yarn
-Polished, hand-selected birch handles

Name: Encore NZ4R Nancy Zeltsman Medium Rattan Marimba Mallets
Description:
The Encore NZ4R Nancy Zeltsman "Medium" mallets are the ideal "tenor" mallet in a graduated set, because of their warm sound. For rolls and chorale playing in the medium-low register, they have a wonderful legato quality. I seldom use these above middle-C. Rattan Handles.

One unique characteristic of my line is that the oval-shape of the mallet head is horizontal rather than vertical. I imagine (and maybe it's true) that the width of the mallet activates more of the vertical grains of wood in every bar-thus contributing to a richer tone. Everything about the construction of my line of mallets-from the cores, to the latex, to the type of yarn-is aimed at helping you to produce a fundamental-rich tone.

- Nancy Zeltsman

Hardness - Medium
Handles - Rattan

Name: Salyers Marching Arts Birch Marimba Mallets - Medium Hard
Description:
The Salyers Percussion MAM30 medium hard yarn mallet works across the entire marimba range. This is a great general mallet choice.

The Salyers Percussion Marching Arts Collection is designed to deliver optimum tone and performance for the Marching Arts activities. The marimba and vibraphone models are wrapped with extra durable yarn and cord to withstand the extra demands of front ensemble playing. All models are designed with extra weight to help achieve the maximum tone projection from the instruments. The unwound models feature 1.25″ brass weighted heads for maximum projection of tone.

Name: Marimba One Colin Currie CCB2 Medium Hard Birch Mallets
Description: The Colin Currie Signature Mallet Series was created as a simple and uncomplicated series of mallets. The series contains four mallets featuring one unwrapped mallet and three wrapped mallets. They allow spontaneity, which can be used with as much force or delicacy without the unsatisfactory change in timbre this approach can create. These mallets represent a focused, polished approach to sound.

Name: Innovative Weichen Lin Marimba Mallets Rattan - Medium
Description:
The Innovative Weichen Lin Medium Marimba Mallets function perfectly across the entire instrument allowing you to achieve clear fundamentals at any dynamic. The blended yarn creates an incredibly versatile mallet, capable of lush Bach chorales as well as more contemporary articulate passages. Built on rattan shafts, the rubber cores provide a strong fundamental tone with very little contact sound.

-16 3/4" Total Length

Name: Encore NZ7B Nancy Zeltsman Best Bass Birch Marimba Mallets
Description:
The Encore NZ7B Nancy Zeltsman "Best Bass" mallets are super-rich bass mallets that produce tones with luscious fundamental in the lowest range. Birch Handles.

One unique characteristic of my line is that the oval-shape of the mallet head is horizontal rather than vertical. I imagine (and maybe it's true) that the width of the mallet activates more of the vertical grains of wood in every bar-thus contributing to a richer tone. Everything about the construction of my line of mallets-from the cores, to the latex, to the type of yarn-is aimed at helping you to produce a fundamental-rich tone.

- Nancy Zeltsman

Hardness - Extra Soft
Handles - Birch

Name: Vic Firth Theodor Milkov Medium Marimba Mallets
Description:
The Vic Firth Theodor Milkov Medium Marimba Mallets is the mallet that delivers a full very defined sound in the lower octave of the marimba. Punchy fundamentals with an even attack.

Length: 17''
Head material/color: Mohair Yarn/Black
Handle Material: Hickory

Name: Vic Firth Virtuoso Series Keyboard Mallets - Soft
Description:
Vic Firth M210 Virtuoso Series Soft Marimba Mallets create a beautiful singing sound in the low register.

Designed for the solo marimba performer, the Vic Firth Virtuoso Series features a rubber core wrapped with a wool blend yarn for an enhanced warm and full-bodied sound. The handles are made of birch with a unique "slip-resistant" finish for excellent feel and enhanced control. A fine choice in ensemble settings as well.

Length: 17"

Name: Malletech Grand Soloist GS16 Marimba Mallet
Description: This Malletech Grand Soloist Series GS16 Marimba Mallet will have a slightly brighter version than the classic GS13.

Name: Innovative Weichen Lin Marimba Mallets Birch - Hard
Description:
The Innovative Weichen Lin Medium Hard Marimba Mallets deliver clear fundamentals with a fast attack, especially in the upper registers of the instrument. The blended yarn creates an incredibly versatile mallet, capable of lush Bach chorales as well as more contemporary articulate passages. Built on birch shafts, the rubber cores provide a strong fundamental tone with very little contact sound.

-16 3/4" Total Length

Name: Marimba One Double Helix Marimba Mallet Bestseller Pack
Description:
The Marimba One Double Helix Marimba Mallets are some of the most sought after mallets for players of all styles. This bundle provides players with 1 pair each of Marimbas One's most popular models at a great price.

The Double Helix Series was developed to bring out the warmth of the fundamental throughout the range of the 5-octave marimba. The relaxed yarn tension on these mallets enhances the smooth, dark attack of the mallet.

The exclusive patent pending “double helix wrap” technique allows you to easily identify each mallet in the series. Two strands of yarn are wrapped independently of each other, creating a woven effect. After one of the strands makes a revolution around the mallet head, the other strand makes a revolution, securing the previous strand. This locks the yarn in place and prevents it from unraveling. This unique method of wrapping provides our mallets with their distinct look, and exceptional sound.

Included Mallets (One Pair Each):
Marimba One Double Helix DHB3 Medium Hard Birch Mallets
Marimba One Double Helix DHB4 Medium Birch Mallets

Name: Salyers Earth Tone Birch Marimba Mallets - Medium Hard
Description:
The Earth Tone Series ET40 medium hard yarn mallet works well as a legato mallet for the upper range of the marimba. This mallet is just hard enough to speak clearly on the upper range with a warm fundamental tone. The natural birch shafts offer superior control to the performer.

The Salyers Percussion Earth Tone Series drew its inspiration from the warm sounds of natural wood and metallic instruments. The yarn wrapped models feature natural birch mallets, 100% soft wool yarn and round rubber cores that create warm and rich organic tones. The rattan models have oval rubber cores and are wrapped in a super durable hemp cord. The Hemp allows these mallets to have a big fundamental tone and the unmistakable sound of a hemp cord wrap.

Name: Malletech Grand Soloist GS3 Marimba Mallet
Description:
The Malletech Grand Soloist GS3 Marimba Mallet represents decades of refinement in marimba mallet design, tracing its roots back to a series that has delivered expansive, full-bodied tone since 1990. Built for players who demand warmth, depth, and consistency, the GS3 reflects Malletech’s meticulous attention to materials and hand-crafted construction.

At the heart of the GS3 is an all-rubber core system, featuring a solid rubber center wrapped in an additional layer of latex. This “all rubber, all the way through” approach creates a rich fundamental tone with exceptional resonance, allowing the marimba to speak freely without harsh overtones. The result is a broad, fat sound that fills the room while maintaining excellent pitch clarity across the instrument’s range.

Each mallet is wound medium-tight using Malletech’s extremely durable proprietary synthetic yarn. This yarn is engineered to balance longevity with tonal sensitivity, producing clean, defined articulation even at very soft dynamic levels. Pianissimo passages remain clear and controlled, making the GS3 equally suitable for expressive solo repertoire and nuanced ensemble playing.

The yarn is applied at a steep winding angle, forming a nearly round mallet head. This precise shape promotes an even contact area on the bars, contributing to consistent tone and response from note to note. Now offered in four carefully calibrated hardness levels, the Grand Soloist GS3 is also available in graduated sets, allowing performers to move seamlessly across registers with a naturally balanced sound.

All-rubber core with added latex layer for a big, warm marimba tone
Medium-tight winding with proprietary synthetic yarn for durability and clarity
Steep-angle winding creates a nearly round head for consistent response
Available in four hardnesses and graduated sets for complete tonal control

Name: Marimba One Katarzyna Mycka KMR3 Medium Hard Rattan Mallets
Description:
The Katarzyna Mycka Professional Mallet Series are wrapped very tightly with all synthetic yarn. The combination of synthetic yarn and the tight wrap gives the immediate response of the bar that is required for ensemble and orchestra playing. The heavy weight of these mallets brings out the warmth of the bars by physically putting into motion the fundamental frequency of the bars.

Katarzyna recommends using this medium hard mallet for such pieces as Keiko Abe's Dream of the Cherry Blossoms (+ one KMB4); Matthias Schmitt's Sechs Miniaturen (6th); Gordon Stout's Two Mexican Dances; Ney Rosauro's Concerto No. 2 (1st Mvmt).

Name: Marimba One Double Helix DHR1 Very Hard Rattan Mallets
Description:
The Double Helix Series was developed to bring out the warmth of the fundamental throughout the range of the 5-octave marimba. The relaxed yarn tension on these mallets enhances the smooth, dark attack of the mallet.

The exclusive patent pending “double helix wrap” technique allows you to easily identify each mallet in the series. Two strands of yarn are wrapped independently of each other, creating a woven effect. After one of the strands makes a revolution around the mallet head, the other strand makes a revolution, securing the previous strand. This locks the yarn in place and prevents it from unraveling. This unique method of wrapping provides our mallets with their distinct look, and exceptional sound.

Name: Marimba One Conrado Moya Birch Marimba Mallets - Medium Soft
Description: The Marimba One Conrado Moya Medium Soft Birch Mallets deliver a clear fundamental with a minimal attack across the entire range of the instrument. Each mallet features a heavy weighted core with tight tensioned yarn. The Moya mallets they achieve clear articulation with a very warm texture, even with the hardest mallets. Designed specifically played with big orchestras, choirs, and percussion ensembles they are also delicate enough to be used in the most intimate chamber music and solo works.

Name: Marimba One Beverley Johnston Hard Marimba Mallets - Birch
Description:
The Marimba One Beverley Johnson Hard Marimba Mallets function perfectly on the upper registrars of the instrument delivering articulate fast fundamentals. This mallets utilizes the Wave Wrap Technology creating a balanced feel as well as allowing it to function on the Vibraphone as well. Like most Marimba One Mallets this series adheres to strict manufacturing standards functions across a wide musical range from the contemporary to the classical.

Hard Mallet Head
Birch Handles

Name: Marimba One Ivana Bilic IBB3 Medium Birch Mallets
Description:
The Marimba One Ivana Bilic model IBB3 Boy Alto mallets are a “shy” mallet that doesn't look for a flashy entrance. It is slightly lighter and softer then IBR5, introspective with a pure and “innocent” sound, and ideal for chamber music and J.S. Bach.

Ivana Bilic wanted her new mallet line to emphasize the singing characteristic of the marimba, by relating the sound that each mallet makes to human vocal qualities. Her definitions for each mallet combine the ideal playing range for a mallet with the color/timbre of its sound.

These mallets are on Birch Handles.

Name: Innovative Artisan Series - Medium Soft Marimba Mallet - Rattan
Description:
The Innovative Artisan Series Medium Soft Marimba Mallet functions across the entire instrument delivering full tonalities with a medium soft attack. Perfect for softer passages that span the entire instrument or a baritone voice in a graduated set.

Medium Soft graduated rubber core
Rattan handles

Name: Innovative Ludwig Albert IP3108B Extra Hard Birch Marimba Mallets
Description:
This line of professional keyboard mallets by Innovative Percussion has been specifically designed for Belgium marimba artist Ludwig Albert III. The eight models, previously only offered on rattan handles, are now available on natural birch handles with specially weighted rubber cores of varying sizes and degrees of hardness.

The IP3108B is an extra hard marimba mallet with birch handles.

Name: Marimba One Double Helix DHR6 Soft Rattan Mallets
Description:
The Double Helix Series was developed to bring out the warmth of the fundamental throughout the range of the 5-octave marimba. The relaxed yarn tension on these mallets enhances the smooth, dark attack of the mallet.

The exclusive patent pending “double helix wrap” technique allows you to easily identify each mallet in the series. Two strands of yarn are wrapped independently of each other, creating a woven effect. After one of the strands makes a revolution around the mallet head, the other strand makes a revolution, securing the previous strand. This locks the yarn in place and prevents it from unraveling. This unique method of wrapping provides our mallets with their distinct look, and exceptional sound.

Name: Innovative Tom Rarick IP3001 Soft Birch Marimba Mallets
Description:
While the contributions of the front ensemble continue to evolve within the marching idiom, so do the demands of the mallets that are used by their highly talented players. Tom Rarick, percussion arranger for the Bluecoats Drum & Bugle Corps, has assisted Innovative Percussion with the design of this unique series of keyboard mallets.

The Tom Rarick Series marimba mallets from Innovative Percussion feature large round cores wrapped with a light grey synthetic wool-blend yarn, while the vibraphone mallets have cord-wrapped large oval cores. All 7 models are built on slightly larger (11/32") polished birch handles. The heavier cores increase projection and enhance the fundamental tone, while the larger handles help balance and control.

IP3001 - Soft Marimba
IP3002 - Medium Soft Marimba
IP3003 - Medium Hard Marimba
IP3004 - Hard Marimba
IP3005 - Soft Vibraphone
IP3006 - Medium Vibraphone
IP3007 - Hard VIbraphone

Name: Innovative Artisan Series - Multi-tone Marimba Mallet - Rattan
Description:
The Innovative Multi-Tone Marimba Mallets allow the player to achieve different levels of articulation without changing mallets. Ideal for performances that feature drastic, sudden dynamic changes.

Multi-Tone yarn wrapped graduated rubber core
Rattan handles

Name: Innovative Soloist Series IP200N Med Soft Birch Marimba Mallets - Natural Handles
Description:
Innovative Percussion's most popular series of solo marimba mallets is now offered with unfinished birch handles thanks to countless requests. All six models are available with a natural finish that provides a more slip resistant grip that is ideal for players who prefer an enhanced grip on their mallets.

The Innovative Percussion IP200N Soloist Series Medium Soft Marimba Mallets are designed for use in the middle 3 octaves of a 5 octave marimba and feature 100% wool yarn and hard rubber cores that help to create a full, rich tome and warm fundamental.

Designed with the marimba virtuoso in mind, the Soloist Series is constructed using hard rubber cores, covered in a series of latex combinations. The mallets are wrapped is a 100% wool wrap, custom manufactured specifically for Innovative Percussion, which delivers a brilliant, rich tone with the warmest fundamental possible in all registers of the marimba.

Name: Innovative Casey Cangelosi CGL1Y Heavy Bass Marimba Mallets
Description:
Wrapped in an environmentally friendly bamboo-blended cord, the Casey Cangelosi Series Marimba Mallets combines the rich, warm tonal characteristics of yarn with the articulate clarity of cord. These mallets are built on birch dowels with a natural finish and allow for a heavy bass sound without sounding muddy or washy, a delicate touch for lyrical legato passages that speak from the mallet immediately, and a steady timbre throughout the dynamic range. The two softest mallets are also available with a soft yarn wrap.

The CGL1Y is a heavy mallet, producing a thick sound that is much less articulate than the CGL1.

Name: Innovative Eriko Daimo Series Medium Hard Marimba Mallets - Yarn Wrapped
Description:
This Innovative Eriko Daimo Series Marimba Mallet features a tightly wrapped rubber core with a custom blended yarn wrap. This model is intended for precise articulate playing with a little less attack than the hard mallets.

Handles - rattan
Hardness- Medium Hard
Length 17"
Orange tape indicator on handle

Name: Salyers Performance Collection Birch Marimba Mallets - Hard
Description:
The Salyers Percussion PCM40 Performer Collection hard marimba mallet is Ideal for use on the upper 3.5 octaves of the 5 octave marimba. The mallets feature a dual surface birch shaft consisting of a natural, unlacquered grip area for maximum control. The upper portion of the shaft portion of the shaft is lacquered for strength and durability. The rubber and latex core is wrapped with a 100% wool yarn.

The Performance Collection marimba mallets were designed with the serious soloist in mind. The collection features rubber cores with varying layers of latex and a wool yarn wrap for a warm full-bodied sound.

Name: Promark SPYR Hard Marimba Mallets
Description:
The Promark SPYR Hard Marimba Mallets function on the upper octaves of a marimba delivering a quick articulate fundamentals. The SM5 is great for fast passages or as a tenor voice in a graduated set.

The Promark SPYR Series uses a proprietary wrapping technique to offer a mallet line that delivers consistency, durability, great tone and projection in every mallet. Consistency like this creates a mallet perfect for high school or college level directors looking to outfit their sections with quality implements for any situation.

Name: Marimba One Katarzyna Mycka KMB7 Very Soft Birch Mallets
Description:
The Katarzyna Mycka Professional Mallet Series are wrapped very tightly with all synthetic yarn. The combination of synthetic yarn and the tight wrap gives the immediate response of the bar that is required for ensemble and orchestra playing. The heavy weight of these mallets brings out the warmth of the bars by physically putting into motion the fundamental frequency of the bars.

Katarzyna recommends using this very soft mallet for such pieces as Andrew Thomas' Merlin (1st Mvmt) and Matthias Schmitt's Sechs Miniaturen (2nd).

Name: Marimba One Round Sound Rattan Marimba Mallets - Medium
Description:
Marimba One Round Sound mallets are the perfect marriage of Marimba One sound quality and the needs of percussionists. The RSR3 is a medium mallet, perfect balance for the middle range, with a rattan handle.

Marimba One Round Sound series mallets feature the same Marimba One excellence at a more affordable price. They are a lightweight mallet with warmth and articulation. This is the mallet that works to make you sound great. Double-wound using Marimba One’s exclusive technology.

Name: Innovative Thomas Burritt TB5 Hard Marimba Mallets
Description:
The Innovative Thomas Burritt TB5 Marimba mallet is the hard mallet of the series. This mallet is designed to be perfect on the upper registrar of the marimba. The TB5 is also a good choice for early marimba, or Japanese Literature. The hard core is loosely wrapped with a soft yarn creating a good balance between attack and bar resonance.
Hard synthetic mallet core
Birch wood handles with a walnut stain
Loosely wrapped mallet core to control attack and enhance timbre

Name: Innovative She-e Wu WU6 Very Hard Birch Marimba Mallets
Description:
The She-e Wu Signature Series Marimba Mallets from Innovative Percussion feature seven mallets of varying hardness, with natural birch handles and a custom blended yarn wrap. Each captures the unique style, sensitivity and essence of the artist that is She-e Wu.

Features:
A combination of hard acrylic-style and hard rubber cores to create a full, bright tone and rich fundamental
Hand-selected unfinished natural birch handles
Wool-blend yarn wrap
Elongated shaped mallet heads

Name: Innovative Soloist Series IP100N Soft Birch Marimba Mallets - Natural Handles
Description:
Innovative Percussion's most popular series of solo marimba mallets is now offered with unfinished birch handles thanks to countless requests. All six models are available with a natural finish that provides a more slip resistant grip that is ideal for players who prefer an enhanced grip on their mallets.

The Innovative Percussion IP100N Soloist Series Soft Marimba Mallets are designed for use in the lower 3 octaves of a 5 octave marimba and feature 100% wool yarn and hard rubber cores that help to create a full, rich tome and warm fundamental.

Designed with the marimba virtuoso in mind, the Soloist Series is constructed using hard rubber cores, covered in a series of latex combinations. The mallets are wrapped is a 100% wool wrap, custom manufactured specifically for Innovative Percussion, which delivers a brilliant, rich tone with the warmest fundamental possible in all registers of the marimba.

Name: Encore 42AYB Yarn Wound Series General Birch Mallets
Description: Introduced in 1982, the Yarn Series mallets were the first latex-wrapped core mallets in history, and have since remained staple implements of percussionists the world over. These are the mallets that set Encore Mallets, Inc. apart from the rest of the industry. Their full, rich, and round sound of maximum fundamental and minimum attack is achieved through the skillful combination of hand-wrapped latex and durable 100% sport wool yarn. For that classic Encore sound, the YR Series is place to start and stay. Medium, with birch shafts.

Name: Yamaha Keiko Abe MKA-03 Hard Rattan Marimba Mallets
Description:
The MKA03 is the Hard Mallet in the Yamaha Keiko Abe Mallet series. Extra weight has been added to the core. To keep the mallet well balanced the Shaft was shortened to 400mm. Good quality yarn is used as a finishing wrap.

The MKA03 will be suited for solo two mallet performances. Perfect for faster more intricate passages that utilize the marimba's upper octaves.

Name: Marimba One Wave Wrap WWXR1 Hard Rattan Mallets
Description: The WWXR1 is a hard mallet with Gray/Finch colored yarn and rattan handles. The highest concentration of yarn is wrapped around the mallet's equator, creating an increased striking surface and a mallet that is more responsive than traditionally wrapped mallets. The Wave Wrap's blend of wool and nylon yarn minimizes contact sound and enhances articulation. Using Wave Wrap technology, the mallet is balanced to facilitate the ease of movement in the player's hand. The double equatorial wrap creates a chevron pattern around the playing surface and a distinctive wave on top of the head - two unique visual signatures for these finest of marimba mallets.

Name: Encore Adam Tan Series Marimba Mallets Birch Handles - Medium Soft
Description:
The Encore Adam Tan Series Marimba Mallets offer a medium weight feel designed for concert solo performances, bringing out the warmth and fundamentals of your marimba sound. The AT Series is comfortable in the hands and plays easily, ideal for all levels and styles of marimba performance.

The mallets feature a latex-wrapped, thick, rubber disk core covered in all natural North American Alpaca wool. The series is also slightly two-toned and extremely versatile in all applications.

AT4 (Medium Soft, Sky Blue) – For warmer, clear sound in the middle to lower registers of the marimba. Works well in the left hand of a graduated set. Great for softer and gentler pieces and chorales.

Name: Vic Firth Theodor Milkov Hard Marimba Mallets
Description:
Vic Firth Theodor Milkov Hard Marimba Mallets delivers a strong fundamental that is bold and articulate. The sound is full and balanced out with a perfect amount of overtones.

Handles - Hickory
Length: 17''
Head material/color: Mohair Yarn/Black

Name: Salyers Jeff Moore JM102 Marimba Mallet - Medium Soft
Description:
The Salyers Jeff Moore JM102 Medium Soft Marimba Mallet features a large weighted core on a birch shaft wrapped in a very durable nylon. This model features enough mass to activate the bar delivering a strong fundamental with a mellow attack. All models work equally well on the concert stage as on the marching field.

Handle – Birch
Hardness – Medium Soft

Name: Malletech MBO12 Mark Boseman Go2 Marimba Mallets
Description: The Malletech MBO12 Mark Boseman Go2 Marimba mallet is ideal for an array of literature where a wide range of dynamics, timbres, and articulations is required without the need to change mallets. The medium-weight oval head combined with a proprietary multi-tonal ‘wrap recipe’ allows the mallets to sing, giving players ultimate expressive control. The design encourages performers to utilize a greater sense of touch to activate a limitless combination of tonal colors. The mallets are medium in weight and produce a lush, warm sound that is darker at lower dynamic levels transitioning to a brighter color at higher dynamic levels.

Name: Malletech Blake Tyson BT22 Marimba Mallets - Hard to Very Hard
Description:
The new line of Blake Tyson mallets offer great expressive potential. They respond to subtle changes of touch, and their depth of sound and sensitive response help to create an amazing variety of timbres and articulation. The Blake Tyson mallets are made with durable wood blend yarn on birch handles.

The BT22 mallets are bright without being brittle; articulate yet still expressive. They can produce brilliant tones in the upper register, but still sound great in the low range. Hard to very hard.

Name: Musser Good Vibes Mallets - Soft (M228)
Description:
Musser Good Vibes mallets have long been sought after and recognized as the world’s finest professional mallets. Handles are choice light rattan carefully selected and matched for size, weight, and flex. Select premium cord is wound around a solid, specially formulated and shaped rubber core, then carefully stitched.

Rattan Handle
Hardness - Soft
Cord Wrapped

Name: Marimba One Beverley Johnston Medium Marimba Mallets - Rattan
Description:
The Marimba One Beverley Johnson Medium Marimba Mallets function perfectly on the across the entire instrument delivering even attack and resonance. This mallets utilizes the Wave Wrap Technology creating a balanced feel as well as allowing it to function on the Vibraphone as well. Like most Marimba One Mallets this series adheres to strict manufacturing standards functions across a wide musical range from the contemporary to the classical.

Medium Mallet Head
Rattan Handles

Name: Innovative William Moersch IP511 - Soft Marimba Mallets
Description:
This William Moersch IP511 Soft Marimba Mallet has been designed exclusively for the marimba virtuoso. The rubber core offers a darker sound than the synthetic core models.

Features:
-rubber cores to create a full, dark tone and rich fundamental
-100% imported wool yarn
-Polished, hand-selected birch handles

Name: Encore NM3R Nanae Mimura Medium Hard Rattan Marimba Mallets
Description: Nanae Mimura Series mallets are slightly heavier than NZ Series mallets. They feature rubber disk cores hand wrapped with latex, and are covered by North American wool. Encore's unique hand wrapped latex process enhances the fundamental allowing harder mallets which do not sound harsh, and improves the feel of the mallet's rebound. The yarn is all natural wool which is not chemically processed and therefore particularly soft and durable. (Our yarn is purchased from the sheep farm that harvests the wool - the yarn is so natural that you may occasionally see a small piece of hay from a mallet head!) The softness of their yarn combined with the hardness and clarity of their latex-wrapped disk cores make NM Series mallets two-toned and very versatile. Medium hard, with rattan shafts.

Name: Balter Ensemble Series 11B Hard Yellow Yarn Mallets - Birch
Description:
The Balter Ensemble Series Birch 11B Hard Yellow Yarn one of the cornerstones of the Mike Balter keyboard mallet line. Description: Two toned: soft and medium hard. Easy to control. “Breaking point” between mezzo-forte and forte.

Description:
The Innovative Weichen Lin Medium Soft Marimba Mallets function perfectly on the lower octaves of the instrument allowing you to achieve clear fundamentals and with some added punch. The blended yarn creates an incredibly versatile mallet, capable of lush Bach chorales as well as more contemporary articulate passages. Built on rattan shafts, the rubber cores provide a strong fundamental tone with very little contact sound.

Name: Malletech Escape Ten EX16 Marimba Mallets
Description: The Malletech Escape Ten EX16 Marimba Mallets are an excellent choice for pieces that require a lot of articulation without being harsh. These mallets project very well through the top of the instrument.

Name: Salyers Performance Collection Birch Marimba Mallets - Legato Medium
Description:
The Salyers Percussion PCM25 Performance Collection Legato Medium Marimba Mallet is ideal for use on the middle 3 octaves of the 5 octave marimba. A good legato general mallet for most general playing situations. The mallets feature a dual surface birch shaft consisting of a natural, unlacquered grip area for maximum control. The upper portion of the shaft portion of the shaft is lacquered for strength and durability. The rubber and latex core is wrapped with a 100% wool yarn.

The Performance Collection marimba mallets were designed with the serious soloist in mind. The collection features rubber and synthetic cores with varying layers of latex and a wool yarn wrap for a warm full-bodied sound.

Name: Marimba One Wave Wrap WWXR5 Soft Rattan Mallets
Description: The WWXR5 is a soft mallet with Gray/Ruby colored yarn and rattan handles. The highest concentration of yarn is wrapped around the mallet's equator, creating an increased striking surface and a mallet that is more responsive than traditionally wrapped mallets. The Wave Wrap's blend of wool and nylon yarn minimizes contact sound and enhances articulation. Using Wave Wrap technology, the mallet is balanced to facilitate the ease of movement in the player's hand. The double equatorial wrap creates a chevron pattern around the playing surface and a distinctive wave on top of the head - two unique visual signatures for these finest of marimba mallets.

Name: Innovative Ludwig Albert IP3102B Med Extra Soft Birch Marimba Mallets
Description:
This line of professional keyboard mallets by Innovative Percussion has been specifically designed for Belgium marimba artist Ludwig Albert III. The eight models, previously only offered on rattan handles, are now available on natural birch handles with specially weighted rubber cores of varying sizes and degrees of hardness.

The IP3102B is a medium extra soft marimba mallet with birch handles.

Name: Marimba One Lynn Vartan LVB1 Hard Birch Marimba Mallets
Description:
The Marimba One Lynn Vartan LVB1 Hard Birch Marimba Mallet features a small round head and is light weight and articulate. This mallet is an extremely versatile hard mallet for any repertoire or venue. Birch handles.

The Marimba One Lynn Vartan LVB1 Hard Birch Marimba Mallet is perfect for fast articulate passages especially in the upper octaves of the Marimba. This mallet also functions well when a faster, staccato attack is required from the lower registrar of the marimba. The Marimba One Lynn Vartan series mallets are "just right" for any grip, any piece, any ensemble, or any venue. The incredibly durable and warm-sounding yarns give clarity, articulation and depth. This versatile series covers your needs from the combo vibe/marimba mallets at the hard end of the range down to the super warm and rich soft mallet. You're sure to find your "just right" fit in this line to add to your quiver!

Name: Innovative Ludwig Albert IP3101B Extra Soft Birch Marimba Mallets
Description:

Name: Encore NM4R Nanae Mimura Medium Rattan Marimba Mallets
Description: Nanae Mimura Series mallets are slightly heavier than NZ Series mallets. They feature rubber disk cores hand wrapped with latex, and are covered by North American wool. Encore's unique hand wrapped latex process enhances the fundamental allowing harder mallets which do not sound harsh, and improves the feel of the mallet's rebound. The yarn is all natural wool which is not chemically processed and therefore particularly soft and durable. (Our yarn is purchased from the sheep farm that harvests the wool - the yarn is so natural that you may occasionally see a small piece of hay from a mallet head!) The softness of their yarn combined with the hardness and clarity of their latex-wrapped disk cores make NM Series mallets two-toned and very versatile. Medium, with rattan shafts.

Name: Salyers Jeff Moore JM104 Marimba Mallet - Hard
Description:
The Salyers Jeff Moore JM104 Hard Marimba Mallet features a large weighted core on a birch shaft wrapped in a very durable nylon. This mallet delivers articulate fundamentals with fast attack perfect for faster aggressive passages. All models work equally well on the concert stage as on the marching field.

Handle – Birch
Hardness – Hard

Description:
The Promark SPYR Soft Marimba Mallets are perfect for lower octaves of the instrument. These deliver a slightly faster attack than the very soft while still offering a full projection. A slightly thicker shaft than traditional marimba mallets offers improved balance and feel.

The Promark SPYR Series uses a proprietary wrapping technique to offer a mallet line that delivers consistency, durability, great tone and projection in every mallet. Consistency like this creates a mallet perfect for high school or college level directors looking to outfit their sections with quality implements for any situation.

Name: Marimba One Ivana Bilic IBB4 Medium Hard Birch Mallets
Description:
Rating
Posted by
Alex
Date Posted
04/25/2011

As mentioned in the Marimba One's description, the IBB4 is indeed quite heavy, especially for a mallet that built for chorales and extending rolling sections. It is labeled as being harder than the IBB3, which I would disagree with-- they are of very similar hardness, only differing in that the IBB4, possessing a larger, more massive head, sounds softer in the top two octaves, and the IBB3, having a smaller head, sounds a little harder in the bottom bars. They are indeed quite bouncy, which helps manipulate them in spite of their weight. I would tend not to use them in the right hand, except for soft pieces (note, however: being tightly wound, they are not ideal for rolling). Overall, they produce an exceptionally powerful, fundamentally-rich tone without being too soft. They can be articulate, especially in the bottom two octaves, without sacrificing sound quality, and are the polar opposite of a brittle mallet.

Name: Promark Andrew Markworth Medium-Hard Marimba Mallets - Birch
Description:

The six models that comprise the Concerto Series have been designed specifically for those players looking for a heavy mallet with a big sound that will project through a large ensemble, but do not want the contact noise that is usually associated with this type of mallet. Compared to standard traditional mallets of similar hardness, the player will immediately notice proportionally more fundamental tone and greater volume. One unique characteristic of my line is that the oval-shape of the mallet head is horizontal rather than vertical. I imagine (and maybe it's true) that the width of the mallet activates more of the vertical grains of wood in every bar-thus contributing to a richer tone. Everything about the construction of my line of mallets-from the cores, to the latex, to the type of yarn-is aimed at helping you to produce a fundamental-rich tone.

Description: Based on the traditional marimba mallets of Mexico and Central America, the Latex Wrapped Series mallets clearly articulate the fundamental pitch of marimba bars. These mallets are offered in five degrees of hardness, and they provide unique color options for marimba as wells as auxiliary percussion. Medium, with rattan shafts.

Name: Yamaha Educational Series Medium Hard Marimba Mallets
Description:
The Yamaha ME202 Medium Marimba Mallets feature Yarn wound core and are ideal for Marimba applications. The Educational Series is designed to deliver a weight and balance the is ideal for students.

Handles - FRP (Fiber Reinforced Plastic)
Length - 15.15"

Name: Yamaha Keiko Abe Signature Birch Keyboard Mallets Hard
Description:
The MKAW03 is the Hard Mallet in the Yamaha Keiko Abe Mallet series. Extra weight has been added to the core. To keep the mallet well balanced the Birch Shaft was shortened to 400mm. Good quality yarn is used as a finishing wrap.

The MKAW03 will be suited for solo two mallet performances. Perfect for faster more intricate passages that utilize the marimba's upper octaves.

Name: Schlagkraft Zivkovic Marimba Mallets - Lilac Tender General (Cedar)
Description:
The Schlagkraft Zivkovic Marimba Mallet NJZ-3 is ideal for a strong attack in low register, but without distracting overtones. Its head (core) is lighter than at NJZ-1 & 2s, so mallet has excellent rebound and feel, especially keeping in mind that independent (Stevens) grip players hold it with two fingers left hand.

The Zivkovich Series Mallets feature rubber cores wrapped in layers of a synthetic yarn. Schlagkraft hand selects and matches the cedar shafts to create a balanced even pair.

Cedar Handles
Medium Soft

Name: Liberty One Scholastic Series Hard Marimba Mallets
Description:
The Liberty One Scholastic Series Marimba Mallets are available in three different head types for all of your playing situations and are great mallets at a great price!

This version is the LMH Hard Yarn model and works well for loud musical passages, particularly in the middle and upper register of the instrument.

Handles - Birch
Hard Yarn

Name: Salyers Earth Tone Rattan Mallets - Medium Hard
Description:
The Earth Tone Series ET90 medium hard hemp cord mallet works equally well on vibraphone or marimba and will be the work horse of the series. The medium hardness of the head will work in fast passages where articulation is required. The rattan handle will have some flex to it and can be used is such techniques as mallet muting.

The hemp wrap provides a unique articulation that works extremely well on marimba for rags, Mexican marimba music and many other styles. The hemp models are also great for marching ensembles due to their extreme durability and ability to project both tone and attack.

The Salyers Percussion Earth Tone Series drew its inspiration from the warm sounds of natural wood and metallic instruments. The yarn wrapped models feature natural birch mallets, 100% soft wool yarn and round rubber cores that create warm and rich organic tones. The rattan models have oval rubber cores and are wrapped in a super durable hemp cord. The Hemp allows these mallets to have a big fundamental tone and the unmistakable sound of a hemp cord wrap.

Name: Promark Discovery Series FPY30 Hard Keyboard Mallets
Description:
The Promark Discovery Series FPY30 Hard Yarn Mallets deliver fast articulation and a comfortable natural feel. When used on Xylophone they offer a full soft tone. 4 layers of a synthetic yarn cover the head while synthetic handles with rubber grips create a mallet with maximum durability.

Length: 12 1/2"
Head Size: 1.2 x 1.38"

Name: Marimba One Ivana Bilic IBR2 Medium Hard Rattan Mallets
Description:
The Marimba One Ivana Bilic model IBR2 Singing Tenor mallets are an expressive solo singing mallet, with a clear but warm and noble sound. It is ideal as a general mallet, adaptive to individual playing style and wide repertoire.

Ivana Bilic wanted her new mallet line to emphasize the singing characteristic of the marimba, by relating the sound that each mallet makes to human vocal qualities. Her definitions for each mallet combine the ideal playing range for a mallet with the color/timbre of its sound.

These mallets are on Rattan Handles.

Name: Innovative Artisan Series - Hard Marimba Mallet - Rattan
Description:
The Innovative Artisan Series Hard Marimba Mallet delivers fast articulate tones with a balanced natural feel. This mallet functions as either the soprano or tenor voice in a graduated set.

Hard graduated rubber core
Rattan handle

Name: Yamaha Keiko Abe Signature Birch Keyboard Mallets Very Soft (MKAWS7)
Description: Designed in close collaboration with Keiko Abe, her Signature Series Mallets are designed to draw out the most from both the performer's technique and tone from the instrument in compositions that require exceptional technique. Ideal for advanced players, these MKAS7 mallets are much heavier than regular mallets. This causes the marimba's tone bars to resonate fully creating a sound that has been up until now, difficult to obtain. The round yarn wound heads are heavier than standard mallets, but they are fully capable of delivering delicate expression. Birch handles.

Name: Yamaha Educational Series Medium Soft Marimba Mallets
Description:
The Yamaha ME203 Medium Soft Marimba Mallets feature Yarn wound core and are ideal for Marimba applications. The Educational Series is designed to deliver a weight and balance the is ideal for students.

Handles - FRP (Fiber Reinforced Plastic)
Length - 15.15"

Name: Marimba One Katarzyna Mycka KMR4 Medium Rattan Mallets
Description:
The Katarzyna Mycka Professional Mallet Series are wrapped very tightly with all synthetic yarn. The combination of synthetic yarn and the tight wrap gives the immediate response of the bar that is required for ensemble and orchestra playing. The heavy weight of these mallets brings out the warmth of the bars by physically putting into motion the fundamental frequency of the bars.

Katarzyna recommends using this medium mallet for such pieces as Keiko Abe's Kazak Lullaby (+ one KMB5); Matthias Schmitt's Corrido; Ney Rosauro's Concerto No. 1 (1st).

Name: Marimba One Lynn Vartan LVB5 Soft Birch Marimba Mallets
Description:
The Marimba One Lynn Vartan LVB5 Soft Birch Marimba Mallet features a medium-large head and is medium-heavy weight. Designed for warmth and depth in sound, this mallet supports your bass but still provides you with heightened articulation in a soft mallet. Birch handles.

The Marimba One Lynn Vartan series mallets are "just right" for any grip, any piece, any ensemble, or any venue. The incredibly durable and warm-sounding yarns give clarity, articulation and depth. This versatile series covers your needs from the combo vibe/marimba mallets at the hard end of the range down to the super warm and rich soft mallet. You're sure to find your "just right" fit in this line to add to your quiver!

Name: Innovative Eriko Daimo Series Hard Marimba Mallets - Yarn Wrapped
Description:
This Innovative Eriko Daimo Series Marimba Mallet features a tightly wrapped rubber core with a custom blended yarn wrap. This model is intended for precise articulate playing.

Handles - rattan
Hardness- Hard
Length 17"
Yellow tape indicator on handle

Name: Encore 14LR Latex Wrapped Medium Soft Rattan Mallets
Description: Based on the traditional marimba mallets of Mexico and Central America, the Latex Wrapped Series mallets clearly articulate the fundamental pitch of marimba bars. These mallets are offered in five degrees of hardness, and they provide unique color options for marimba as wells as auxiliary percussion. Medium soft, with rattan shafts

Name: Encore 13LR Latex Wrapped Medium Hard Rattan Mallets
Description: Based on the traditional marimba mallets of Mexico and Central America, the Latex Wrapped Series mallets clearly articulate the fundamental pitch of marimba bars. These mallets are offered in five degrees of hardness, and they provide unique color options for marimba as wells as auxiliary percussion. Medium hard, with rattan shafts.

Name: Dragonfly Percussion Marimba Mallets Medium Soft with Rattan Handles
Description: This is the Dragonfly Percussion Medium Soft Rattan Marimba Mallet. Perfect for the lower octaves of a marimba, or the bass mallet in a graduated set, it results in a lot of fundamental and clear attack. The selected rubber core, cloth covering and quality rattan handle make this a very playable mallet.

Name: Balter Ensemble Series 16B Extra Soft Orange Yarn Mallets - Birch
Description:
The Balter Ensemble Series Birch 16B Extra Soft Orange Yarn is one of the cornerstone of the Mike Balter keyboard mallet line. These single-tone yarn wound mallets feature solid rubber cores that are the ideal weight for general playing. Their proven reliability has made them an industry standard that continues to exceed the needs of the player.

Type: Orange Yarn
Handle: Birch
Hardness: Extra Soft
Use: Marimba

Name: Vic Firth Anders Astrand Blue Series M303 Medium Hard Keyboard Mallets
Description:
The Vic Firth Anders Astrand Blue Series M303 Medium Hard Keyboard Mallets perform across the entire range of both vibraphone and marimba delivering clear articulate tones.

The Vic Firth Anders Astrand Blue Series features an increased weight adding depth and projection. The Blue Series performs in solo situations because the weight of the mallet does the work so the performer plays through the parts in a natural relaxed manner.

Head - Cord
Handles - Rattan

Name: Salyers Earth Tone Birch Marimba Mallets - Medium Soft
Description:
The Earth Tone Series ET20 medium soft yarn mallet works great for any legato playing, transcriptions or chorales. The natural birch shafts offer superior control to the performer.

The Salyers Percussion Earth Tone Series drew its inspiration from the warm sounds of natural wood and metallic instruments. The yarn wrapped models feature natural birch mallets, 100% soft wool yarn and round rubber cores that create warm and rich organic tones. The rattan models have oval rubber cores and are wrapped in a super durable hemp cord. The Hemp allows these mallets to have a big fundamental tone and the unmistakable sound of a hemp cord wrap.

Name: Encore DW2 Doug Walter Medium Rattan Marimba Mallets
Description:
Encore DW2 Doug Walter Marimba Mallets feature Medium Heads and Rattan Handles. Designed in conjunction with Dr. Douglas Walter, Professor of Percussion for the University of Colorado at Boulder, the DW Series offers percussionists articulation and full timbre on both marimba and vibraphone.

DW1, DW2, and DW3 mallets features soft, nylon-wool yarn for decreased attack on the marimba. DW11, DW12, and DW13 mallets features super strong synthetic cord for increased articulation on vibraphone. All DW mallets can be used interchangeably on marimba and vibraphone.

Name: Marimba One Round Sound Rattan Marimba Mallets - Medium Soft
Description:
Marimba One Round Sound mallets are the perfect marriage of Marimba One sound quality and the needs of percussionists. The RSR4 is a medium soft mallet, lightweight yet produces a rich and articulate bass sound, with a rattan handle.

Marimba One Round Sound series mallets feature the same Marimba One excellence at a more affordable price. They are a lightweight mallet with warmth and articulation. This is the mallet that works to make you sound great. Double-wound using Marimba One’s exclusive technology.

Name: Marimba One Conrado Moya Birch Marimba Mallets - Soft
Description: The Marimba One Conrado Moya Soft Birch Mallets deliver a clear fundamental with a very little attack especially in the low range of the instrument. Each mallet features a heavy weighted core with tight tensioned yarn. The Moya mallets they achieve clear articulation with a very warm texture, even with the hardest mallets. Designed specifically played with big orchestras, choirs, and percussion ensembles they are also delicate enough to be used in the most intimate chamber music and solo works.

Name: Innovative Artisan Series - Very Hard Marimba Mallet - Cedar
Description:
The Innovative Artisan Series Very Hard Marimba Mallet functions particularly good on the upper registrars of the instrument. Very hard mallets are perfect for fast articulate passages and when added punch is needed on lower octave notes.

Very hard graduated rubber core
Cedar handles

Name: Encore NM5R Nanae Mimura Medium Soft Rattan Marimba Mallets
Description: Nanae Mimura Series mallets are slightly heavier than NZ Series mallets. They feature rubber disk cores hand wrapped with latex, and are covered by North American wool. Encore's unique hand wrapped latex process enhances the fundamental allowing harder mallets which do not sound harsh, and improves the feel of the mallet's rebound. The yarn is all natural wool which is not chemically processed and therefore particularly soft and durable. (Our yarn is purchased from the sheep farm that harvests the wool - the yarn is so natural that you may occasionally see a small piece of hay from a mallet head!) The softness of their yarn combined with the hardness and clarity of their latex-wrapped disk cores make NM Series mallets two-toned and very versatile. Medium soft, with rattan shafts.

Name: Vic Firth Theodor Milkov Hard & Bright Marimba Mallets
Description:
Vic Firth Theodor Milkov Hard & Bright Marimba Mallets delivers a full crystal clear tone. The sound produced offers an articulation that leaves no questions unanswered.

Length: 17''
Head material/color: Mohair Yarn/Black
Handle Material: Hickory

Name: Salyers Percussion Kai Stensgaard Signature Marimba Mallet - Medium
Description:
Salyers Percussion Kai Stensgaard Signature Medium Marimba Mallets are designed by world-renowned marimba artist Kai Stensgaard. This series features natural/unfinished birch shafts, rubber cores and a wool yarn wrap. The five models are graduated in hardness and weight. Kai is an accomplished 6-mallet player and has designed his mallets to be very manageable using either 4 or 6 mallet grip. The sound throughout the series is clear and warm. The white yarn provides excellent visual awareness for the performer and each model is color-coded for quick and easy identification.

Although designed for the professional player, many teachers and students prefer these mallets for their traditional weight and sound.

Name: Malletech Michael Burritt Version 2 MB13-V2 Marimba Mallets - Medium to Medium Hard Rattan
Description:
Multi-toned: medium to medium hard with red label.

Provides a full sound in all the registers and rolls well at medium dynamic levels. This is a great general mallet for those players who want the sound of a rubber core and a bit of bite to the attack.

Mike is internationally respected as a marimba virtuoso, composer and teacher. The six models of the Michael Burritt Series are built around 1 1/8″ diameter natural rubber cores. All of the models also have one or more additional layers of latex rubber on top of the natural rubber for a more complex multi-toned response pattern. All models are wrapped and stitched with a synthetic blend that has good durability. The yarn has also been designed to have just a touch of contact “chiff” for the extra cutting power Michael requires. All models feature selected rattan shafts.

Synthetic Yarn
Medium Hard to Medium Hard Hardness (Multi-tonal)
Rattan Shafts
Oval Shaped Mallet Head

Name: Marimba One Colin Currie CCB4 Soft Birch Mallets
Description: The Colin Currie Signature Mallet Series was created as a simple and uncomplicated series of mallets. The series contains four mallets featuring one unwrapped mallet and three wrapped mallets. They allow spontaneity, which can be used with as much force or delicacy without the unsatisfactory change in timbre this approach can create. These mallets represent a focused, polished approach to sound.

Name: Innovative Soloist Series IP400N Hard Birch Marimba Mallets - Natural Handles
Description:
Innovative Percussion's most popular series of solo marimba mallets is now offered with unfinished birch handles thanks to countless requests. All six models are available with a natural finish that provides a more slip resistant grip that is ideal for players who prefer an enhanced grip on their mallets.

The Innovative Percussion IP400N Soloist Series Hard Marimba Mallets are designed for use in the upper 3 octaves of a 5 octave marimba and offers lots of strong articuation. This model features 100% wool yarn and hard rubber cores that help to create a full, rich tome and warm fundamental.

Designed with the marimba virtuoso in mind, the Soloist Series is constructed using hard rubber cores, covered in a series of latex combinations. The mallets are wrapped is a 100% wool wrap, custom manufactured specifically for Innovative Percussion, which delivers a brilliant, rich tone with the warmest fundamental possible in all registers of the marimba.

Name: Malletech Escape Ten Phat Bass EX5R Marimba Mallets
Description: The Malletech Escape Ten Phat Bass EX5R Marimba Mallets are a special weighted mallet on a rattan handle. The EX5R is designed to provide superb fundamentals and presence that speaks well into the upper-mid register.

Name: Encore NZ1R Nancy Zeltsman Hard Rattan Marimba Mallets
Description:
The Encore NZ1R Nancy Zeltsman "Hard" mallets are very bright, and ideal for cutting through while retaining a "marimba tone" (as opposed to a xylophone tone). They sound particularly clear and beautiful for soft playing in the highest register. Rattan Handles.

One unique characteristic of my line is that the oval-shape of the mallet head is horizontal rather than vertical. I imagine (and maybe it's true) that the width of the mallet activates more of the vertical grains of wood in every bar-thus contributing to a richer tone. Everything about the construction of my line of mallets-from the cores, to the latex, to the type of yarn-is aimed at helping you to produce a fundamental-rich tone.

- Nancy Zeltsman

Hardness - Hard
Handles - rattan

Name: Salyers Percussion Kai Stensgaard Signature Marimba Mallets - Medium Hard
Description:
Salyers Percussion Kai Stensgaard Signature Medium Hard Marimba Mallets are designed by world-renowned marimba artist Kai Stensgaard. This series features natural/unfinished birch shafts, rubber cores and a wool yarn wrap. The five models are graduated in hardness and weight. Kai is an accomplished 6-mallet player and has designed his mallets to be very manageable using either 4 or 6 mallet grip. The sound throughout the series is clear and warm. The white yarn provides excellent visual awareness for the performer and each model is color-coded for quick and easy identification.

Although designed for the professional player, many teachers and students prefer these mallets for their traditional weight and sound.

Name: Marimba One Ivana Bilic IBB6 Medium Soft Birch Mallets
Description:
The Marimba One Ivana Bilic model IBB6 Power Bass mallets combine weight, and robust and rich sound, with articulation. It is heavier and harder than the IB5, and makes the low C really ring. Well suited for legato, rolled or fast passages, it sounds particularly nice in the two lowest octaves, enhancing the fundamental with a powerful, vibrant and clear sound. Ivana recommends it as the bass mallet for a combined set, with two IB5's or IB5 + IB4 as the two inner mallets. The combination of softer mallets with IB6 as the bass mallet works very well for chords (with or without rolls) because it emphasizes the bass line, and gives to the chord the right harmonic quality.

Ivana Bilic wanted her new mallet line to emphasize the singing characteristic of the marimba, by relating the sound that each mallet makes to human vocal qualities. Her definitions for each mallet combine the ideal playing range for a mallet with the color/timbre of its sound.

These mallets are on Birch Handles.

Name: Marimba One Ivana Bilic IBB1 Very Hard Birch Mallets
Description:
The Marimba One Ivana Bilic model IBB1 High Soprano mallets are light and piercing, with crisp clear sound and articulation. It is designed for virtuoso passages in the upper register and aggressive contemporary-styled pieces.

Ivana Bilic wanted her new mallet line to emphasize the singing characteristic of the marimba, by relating the sound that each mallet makes to human vocal qualities. Her definitions for each mallet combine the ideal playing range for a mallet with the color/timbre of its sound.

These mallets are on Birch Handles.

Name: Innovative She-e Wu WU6C Very Hard Birch Concerto Marimba Mallets
Description: The WU6C Very Hard Concerto model is an addition to the She-e Wu Series marimba mallets. This model is constructed much like the existing WU6, but is tightly wrapped with cord. This helps produce a bright, staccato timbre that speaks very quickly and will help cut through a large ensemble.

Name: Innovative Nick Mancini NM1 Medium Hard Vibraphone Mallets
Description:
The NM1 Nick Mancini vibraphone mallets were designed to be used in a variety of musical settings. Created with the versatile musician in mind, they are considered a multi-use mallet, making them perfect for someone looking to explore a wide variety of styles.

Made with extra-durable rattan handles and a large oval shaped rubber core, the player can easily transfer a great amount of energy into the bar with less effort. The mallet is wrapped with a sage green tightly woven multi-ply cord, and stitched with an orange accent yarn.

Features:
Extra-Durable Rattan Handles
Large Oval Shaped Rubber Cores
Thightly Woven Multi-ply Cord
Orange Accent Stitching

Name: JGpercussion Matthew Coley MC4B Marimba Mallets - Medium Soft
Description:
The JGpercussion Matthew Coley Signature Series Marimba Mallets and wrapped with high quality alpaca yarn.

Model MC4B - Medium Soft
Birch Handles

Name: Encore 23LB Latex Wrapped Medium Hard Birch Mallets
Description: Based on the traditional marimba mallets of Mexico and Central America, the Latex Wrapped Series mallets clearly articulate the fundamental pitch of marimba bars. These mallets are offered in five degrees of hardness, and they provide unique color options for marimba as wells as auxiliary percussion. Medium hard, with birch shafts.

Name: Encore NZ6R Nancy Zeltsman Articulate Bass Rattan Marimba Mallets
Description:
The Encore NZ6R Nancy Zeltsman "Articulate Bass" mallets are a very rich bass mallet for general playing, with a bit more articulation than the NZ7. I use this as my bass mallet about 75% of the time. Rattan Handles.

One unique characteristic of my line is that the oval-shape of the mallet head is horizontal rather than vertical. I imagine (and maybe it's true) that the width of the mallet activates more of the vertical grains of wood in every bar-thus contributing to a richer tone. Everything about the construction of my line of mallets-from the cores, to the latex, to the type of yarn-is aimed at helping you to produce a fundamental-rich tone.

- Nancy Zeltsman

Hardness - Soft
Handles - Rattan

Name: Encore NM7B Nanae Mimura Very Soft Birch Marimba Mallets
Description: Nanae Mimura Series mallets are slightly heavier than NZ Series mallets. They feature rubber disk cores hand wrapped with latex, and are covered by North American wool. Encore's unique hand wrapped latex process enhances the fundamental allowing harder mallets which do not sound harsh, and improves the feel of the mallet's rebound. The yarn is all natural wool which is not chemically processed and therefore particularly soft and durable. (Our yarn is purchased from the sheep farm that harvests the wool - the yarn is so natural that you may occasionally see a small piece of hay from a mallet head!) The softness of their yarn combined with the hardness and clarity of their latex-wrapped disk cores make NM Series mallets two-toned and very versatile. Very soft, with birch shafts.

Name: Yamaha Keiko Abe MKA-02 Very Hard Rattan Marimba Mallets
Description:
This is the Very Hard Mallet from the Yamaha Keiko Abe Mallet Series (MKA02). The Core has extra weight added to increase the hardness but to compensate the Rattan shaft has been shortened to 400mm to provide for excellent balance. A high quality yarn has been used as the final wrap in this mallet.

This Mallet will serve well as a solo two mallet performance mallet. Perfect for faster intricate passages in the upper octaves of the marimba.

Name: Salyers Performance Collection Rattan Mallets - Soft Rubber
Description:
The Salyers Percussion PCX41 soft rubber mallet works well on marimba or xylophone. The rattan shafts are matched in diameter and flex.

The Performance Collection unwound mallets are designed to offer a comprehensive selection of sonic possibilities to the advanced percussionist. Head sizes available are 1″, 1.125″ and 1.25″. The rattan size sets this series apart from any other series available. The trend of using very thick rattan on unwound mallets results in a stiff shaft that does not allow the natural flex of rattan to help get the best sound from the keys. We use a rattan size that feels good yet flexes just enough to help you get the best sound possible from your instruments.

Name: Marimba One Katarzyna Mycka KMR2 Hard Rattan Mallets
Description:
The Katarzyna Mycka Professional Mallet Series are wrapped very tightly with all synthetic yarn. The combination of synthetic yarn and the tight wrap gives the immediate response of the bar that is required for ensemble and orchestra playing. The heavy weight of these mallets brings out the warmth of the bars by physically putting into motion the fundamental frequency of the bars.

Katarzyna recommends using this hard mallet for such pieces as Leander Kaiser's Hurricane's Eye; Ney Rosauro's Marimba concerto No. 1 (3rd Mvmt, 2nd Mvmt - right hand), No. 2 (3rd Mvmt); Andrew Thomas's Merlin (2nd Mvmt); John Thrower's Aurora borealis (1st Mvmt); Anders Koppel's Concerto No. 1 (3rd Mvmt); and Toshimitsu Tanaka's Two Movements (1st).

Name: Marimba One Katarzyna Mycka KMR1 Very Hard Rattan Mallets
Description:
The Katarzyna Mycka Professional Mallet Series are wrapped very tightly with all synthetic yarn. The combination of synthetic yarn and the tight wrap gives the immediate response of the bar that is required for ensemble and orchestra playing. The heavy weight of these mallets brings out the warmth of the bars by physically putting into motion the fundamental frequency of the bars.

Katarzyna recommends using this very hard mallet for such pieces as Eckhard Kopetzki's Etude Hommage, Concerto for Marimba and Strings (4th Mvmt).

Name: Marimba One Lynn Vartan LVR Hard Rattan Vibraphone Mallets
Description:
The Marimba One Lynn Vartan Vibraphone Mallet features a small mushroom head and is light weight and highly articulate. This mallet could be used as a hard articulate mallet for marimba or for a great vibraphone solo or ensemble sound! Rattan handles.

The Marimba One Lynn Vartan series mallets are "just right" for any grip, any piece, any ensemble, or any venue. The incredibly durable and warm-sounding yarns give clarity, articulation and depth. This versatile series covers your needs from the combo vibe/marimba mallets at the hard end of the range down to the super warm and rich soft mallet. You're sure to find your "just right" fit in this line to add to your quiver!

Name: Innovative Mark Ford Rhapsody Series - IP824 Very Hard Marimba Mallets
Description:
Innovative Mark Ford Rhapsody Series Concerto Hard Marimba Mallets are designed to deliver a brighter more articulate sound than other Mark Ford Mallets. The Very Hard Mallet is perfect for upper octaves of the instrument delivering the fastest and most articulate tonalities in the series. It functions well as the soprano roll in graduated set.

Very hard mallet core
Birch handles

Name: Encore 23ALB Latex Wrapped Medium Birch Mallets
Description: Based on the traditional marimba mallets of Mexico and Central America, the Latex Wrapped Series mallets clearly articulate the fundamental pitch of marimba bars. These mallets are offered in five degrees of hardness, and they provide unique color options for marimba as wells as auxiliary percussion. Medium, with birch shafts.

Name: Dragonfly Percussion Marimba Mallets Hard with Rattan Handles
Description: The Hard Mallet from Dragonfly's Marimba Mallet Series plays very clear and accurate tones without sounding harsh or brittle. Being the treble mallet in a graduated set of solo playing on the top four octaves of a marimba is where this mallet feels right at home.

Name: Schlagkraft Zivkovic Marimba Mallets - Deep Ocean Blue (Rattan)
Description:
The Deep Ocean Schlagkraft Zivkovic Marimba Mallet delivers a deep, clear sound, with a clear attack. The NJZ-2 allows larger sized bars to ring (Yamaha 6000, Malletech, and Musser). Many players love this mallet for Bach’s slow movements or any music where resonant, tender and rich single notes are needed.

The Zivkovich Series Mallets feature rubber cores wrapped in layers of a synthetic yarn. Schlagkraft hand selects and matches the rattan shafts to create a balanced even pair.

Rattan Handles
Soft

Name: Schlagkraft Zivkovic Marimba Mallets - Deep Ocean Blue (Cedar)
Description:
The Deep Ocean Schlagkraft Zivkovic Marimba Mallet delivers a deep, clear sound, with a clear attack. The NJZ-2 allows larger sized bars to ring (Yamaha 6000, Malletech, and Musser). Many players love this mallet for Bach’s slow movements or any music where resonant, tender and rich single notes are needed.

The Zivkovich Series Mallets feature rubber cores wrapped in layers of a synthetic yarn. Schlagkraft hand selects and matches the cedar shafts to create a balanced even pair.

Cedar Handles
Soft

Name: Salyers Makoto Nakura Marimba/Vibraphone Mallets - Medium
Description:
The Salyers Makoto Nakura MN3 is a medium mallet and works in many 4 mallet solo and chamber works. Medium hard mallets are able to deliver a wide range sounds. It features rattan handles and works equally well on marimba or vibraphone.

Hardness – Medium
Handles - Rattan

Name: Salyers Performance Collection Birch Marimba Mallets - Medium Soft
Description:
The Salyers Percussion PCM20 Performer Collection medium soft marimba mallet is ideal for use on the lower 3.5 octaves of the 5 octave marimba. The mallets feature a dual surface birch shaft consisting of a natural, unlacquered grip area for maximum control. The upper portion of the shaft portion of the shaft is lacquered for strength and durability. The rubber and latex core is wrapped with a 100% wool yarn.

The Performance Collection marimba mallets were designed with the serious soloist in mind. The collection features rubber cores with varying layers of latex and a wool yarn wrap for a warm full-bodied sound.

Name: Encore 32YR Yarn Wound Series Medium Hard Rattan Mallets
Description: Introduced in 1982, the Yarn Series mallets were the first latex-wrapped core mallets in history, and have since remained staple implements of percussionists the world over. These are the mallets that set Encore Mallets, Inc. apart from the rest of the industry. Their full, rich, and round sound of maximum fundamental and minimum attack is achieved through the skillful combination of hand-wrapped latex and durable 100% sport wool yarn. For that classic Encore sound, the YR Series is place to start and stay. Medium hard, with rattan shafts.

Name: Encore 24LB Latex Wrapped Medium Soft Birch Mallets
Description: Based on the traditional marimba mallets of Mexico and Central America, the Latex Wrapped Series mallets clearly articulate the fundamental pitch of marimba bars. These mallets are offered in five degrees of hardness, and they provide unique color options for marimba as wells as auxiliary percussion. Medium soft, with birch shafts.

Name: Yamaha Keiko Abe Signature Birch Keyboard Mallets Extra Soft
Description: The MKAW09 is the Extra Soft Mallet from the Keiko Abe Mallet line. It features a rubber core with a 2mm tube wrapped around it. The softest of high quality yarn is used as the finishing wrap. The 430mm Straight Birch shaft is selected to create a perfectly balanced mallet for the more delicate passages of music. This mallet will excel when the performer wants no bar contact sound. Perfect as a bass mallet in a graduated set.

Name: Vic Firth Anders Astrand Blue Series M304 Hard Keyboard Mallets
Description:
The Vic Firth Anders Astrand Blue Series M304 Hard Keyboard Mallets deliver bright articulate tone on either marimba or vibraphone. This is a good selection for performances that require bright articulation.

The Vic Firth Anders Astrand Blue Series features an increased weight adding depth and projection. The Blue Series performs in solo situations because the weight of the mallet does the work so the performer plays through the parts in a natural relaxed manner.

Head - Cord
Handles - Rattan

Name: Vic Firth Anders Astrand Blue Series M302 Medium Keyboard Mallets
Description:
The Vic Firth Anders Astrand Blue Series M302 Medium Keyboard Mallets perform across the entire range of the marimba and vibraphone. These are ideal for solo or small ensemble.

The Vic Firth Anders Astrand Blue Series features an increased weight adding depth and projection. The Blue Series performs in solo situations because the weight of the mallet does the work so the performer plays through the parts in a natural relaxed manner.

Head - Cord
Handles - Rattan

Name: Salyers Makoto Nakura Marimba/Vibraphone Mallets - Medium Hard
Description:
The Salyers Makoto Nakura MN4 is a super versatile mallet that works well in solo or concerto pieces. It features rattan shafts and works equally well on marimba and vibraphone.

Hardness – Medium Hard
Handles – Rattan

Name: Innovative Sandi Rennick Series Hard Marimba Mallets - Sage Green Cord
Description:
The IP4000C series features cord-wound marimba mallets designed to the specifications of Sandi Rennick, the Front Ensemble Arranger of the 9-time DCI World Champion Santa Clara Vanguard. Known for putting more weight in the players hands, these models produce a focused sound which speaks quickly, and was created with today's modern amplified keyboard section in mind. The addition of a cord-wrapped option adds enhanced articulation and a new voice for the front ensemble.

Starting with durable birch handles in a matte finish, these implements are wrapped with cord allowing them to be articulate and cutting while withstanding the needs of rigorous rehearsal and performance schedules. The versatility found in the Sandi Rennick series makes balancing and blending your ensemble easier than ever before.

Sandi Rennick Series

IP4001C: Soft Marimba - Cord
